### Kathy Spears — 2019-02-27T05:15:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-264026>

Hi! I'm brand new here. My head is spinning with all the info I'm trying to understand! I'm so thankful for this site.

My health is very complicated. Here goes:

I'm 57, live in Nashville, TN; originally from LA, CA; lived in Stockholm, Sweden for 6 years. I have type 1 diabetes (since age 35), Hashimoto's (age 22), pernicious anemia, Reynaud's, and alopecia areata (remission for last 18 yrs!), high blood pressure and high LDL cholesterol (which I don't worry about).

Both of my sisters have HUS - hemolytic uremic syndrome. One sister had 4 kidney transplants but passed away from congestive heart failure secondary to too many years of dialysis causing severely leaking valves that couldn't be replaced because of her health. I gave her a kidney at age 22. It was done at Vanderbilt and when the PA examined me to release me after the surgery she noticed that I had a thyroid goiter; eventually diagnosed as Hashimoto's which made me unable to be a kidney donor to my sister. Did I say this was AFTER the surgery! I started piling on the autoimmune diseases after that.

My father was a marine stationed at Camp Lejeune, NC before he married and had kids. There was an ongoing water contamination of cancer-causing chemicals, including industrial solvents, benzene, and other toxins during his time there. There has been a class action law suit and as many as 1 million marines were affected. My father died at age 54 of AML - one of the leukemias on the list of diseases caused by the chemicals. I believe that my sisters and I have many serious health issues as a result of his exposure.

I started a ketogenic diet almost 2 years ago and lost about 35 lbs. I have switched to paleo keto (Paleomedicina protocol) in an effort to stop the inflammation in my body. My latest HS CRP was 4.3 Not good. Also have had a few bouts of what could be the beginning of giant cell arteritis but I refuse to get the biopsy because I believe that I can stop it in its tracks if I just find the right path. I have found my move to low-zero carbs has been only positive. Haven't rerun the CRP yet.

I don't believe in dwelling on past medical errors that have led to my health circumstances. I only want to spend time and energy on solutions.

My mother has had type 2 diabetes for many years and suffered 2 heart attacks and a stroke before moving to be close to me. When she got here she looked about 90 (was actually 73) and I didn't think she'd live long. I have had her following a low carb diet and she is now off all her diabetes meds. I took her off her statins and most of her other meds. I don't dare stop the blood pressure and blood thinners they have her on but I did stop the baby aspirin. She lost 65lbs, regained almost full cognition, and is doing very well. I started her on D3 & K2 (with permission from her doc) in an effort to slow down the atherosclerosis. I'm trying desperately to get her outside into the sun but she complains of the pain in her eyes from the sun. She has macular degeneration and is almost blind. I don't know if any of this will help but she seems 20 years younger!

I have 2 sons with health issues of their own. Thanks to me. This is why I spend every waking moment when not working (I am an esthetician and just sold my spa because I've lost my passion for the industry and now I'm all in on wellness) studying everything I can. Oldest son has ADHD, OCD, asthma, & anxiety. Youngest has severe food allergies (wheat, dairy, eggs, fish, nuts ++), and asthma. I am desperate to keep both of them from developing all the diseases that seem to be imminent. I haven't yet figured out how to get good DHA into my son with the fish allergies. I'm afraid that if I don't figure all this EMF out soon, the cascade of diseases will begin for them. I am currently forcing them both to watch the "Resonance - Beings of Frequency" film so they will begin to take me seriously with the cell phones and all the rest of the environmental problems.

So, if you're still here reading, I send you a big hug and huge thank you. I look for any and all ideas and advice.
Kathy

### Kathy Spears — 2019-02-27T15:39:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-264045>

Thanks Jack. Very interesting study. It's hard to accept that all of my "bad" DNA can be balanced by some sunshine and nnEMF avoidance. Is it really that simple/possible?

I tend towards all or nothing. I got outside at sunup today but only had 10 mins. Is this better than nothing? Is it enough to be barefoot on my aggregate patio or do I need my feet on the earth? I tend to get buried in the details. I am trying to go outside between each client I see at work. That might just be 5 minutes each time.

I am now thinking about all of my machinery that I use in my esthetic practice. Am I exposed to more trouble with my micro current machine? High Intense Focused Ultrasound machine? Galvanic current spot treatment machine? I use a bright LED light for certain treatments - BAD! I obviously have no understanding of electricity so I don't even really know what is doing what to me. I also have a machine that delivers ultrasound, radio frequency, infrared and "biomechanical" vibrations. Is the infrared beneficial in this type of delivery? It is a hand held device that must be moved along the skin. I never take the time to use any of my equipment on myself. This machine might be worth the time??

I'm sorry for asking so many stupid questions. I plan on becoming a paying member but feel I should learn as much as possible to get up to speed first. Right now I feel I have way too much to digest. I'm obviously not as smart as most of your members!

I am so thankful that I stumbled upon you, Jack, and this amazing resource. Wish I wasn't so late to the party! Your time and energy given to all of us is of value beyond what we could pay at any price.

### Kathy Spears — 2019-03-03T03:15:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-264327>

I wonder if any of you super smart people can help with something. I have been following a low carb (< 30 g/day) hi fat diet with intermittent fasting for about a year and a half and then the past 6 weeks have converted to meat only in an effort to stop all the inflammation in my body. I've lost about 35 lbs but when my most recent labs were done, my HS CRP was 4.3 (yikes!) and my homocysteine was also high. I have Hashimoto's and T1D + lots more. My thyroid peroxidase was also very elevated. My question: Do I still have a leptin problem? Even after such a long time of eating better? I never eat breakfast as I've been fasting 16 hrs daily and wonder if I need to start eating breakfast as Jack recommends. I've also been taking Omega 3 off and on. Do I need to scrap my Paleo Keto diet and start the Leptin RX? I only need to add early morning eating to make that happen and can't believe that would make a difference. Please help!! Thanks!!

### Marko Pollo — 2019-03-03T04:51:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-264329>

Im not super smart, but here's my perspective. If you felt great and were only doing this for shits and giggles, probably wouldnt matter what or when you ate for breakfast. But since as you put it "my HS CRP was 4.3 (yikes!) and my homocysteine was also high. I have Hashimoto's and T1D + lots more.My thyroid peroxidase was alsovery elevated" , I would follow the protocols as close to verbatim as possible. I believe we do ourselves a great disservice when we become sea lawyers trying to negotiate the protocols to fit conveniently into our present lives instead of changing our habits. I would also prefer skipping breakfast but the timing and size of the BAB is foundational for the leptin RX , circadian timing and just about everything else on here. Good luck

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-05T05:54:47+0200

DDW would help most Hashi Type 1 D........but without getting the environment right it might not be a wise first step financially.

How do you touch others in your own life? Do you use your hands? Your words? Your ideas? Is touch physical for you or is it used wirelessly by touching without touch?

Must it be used in close proximity or can your ability to reach people to expand? Can you connect well to anyone anywhere and any time you want?

Are touch and time connected in biology? I think they are.

The invisible threads nature uses to connect to us are the strongest ties we touch others with.

Touch is a very complex thing that is intoxicating and intangible for most. Friends might lose touch but they never seem to lose feelings........Friends tend to misplace their power to touch us. Even wounded animals touch each other to heal and surgeons have to touch their patients to heal them. The sun has to touch us to regenerate ourselves to live. Touch is incredibly complex and how we apply it or misuse it defines our lives. Are you being touched right now digitally on the internet?

### Kathy Spears — 2019-03-07T15:39:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-264697>

I just heard Jack say, on a talk he did in August of 2018, that part of the reason he left Nashville was due to the Google "5G rings" going up; and that Nashville was becoming one of the cities with early 5G rollout. More than ever, I have to find someplace better for myself and my sons.

I have been failing miserably lately with the sunrise watching and sun exposure. It is F'ing freezing out and I am a wuss. I walk out, stand there for a minute, and walk back inside because I don't feel like there is any point when I'm not getting the rays I am craving. I know it's just excuses. Is there any point in even going out if it's just for a minute for my eyes to see the rising sun's rays? Is 60 seconds better than nothing?

I will be out for 3 hours starting at 4:30 because my son has 2 baseball scrimmages. However, I will be covered in lots of layer of clothing as the sun goes down because it is usually below freezing and only gets colder. All of this just seems pointless until I can get lower in latitude.

I'll be in Florida in 2 weeks for a baseball tournament for 1 week. I am really hoping for good, warm weather. This time of year it is a toss up.

My "husband" (housemate, not even roommate) won't discuss any of this because he is very aware of the nnEMF issues but believes there's nothing that can be done so he doesn't want to even think or talk about it. He is not "in the game" at all. We are getting our house ready to put on the market so I will be in a situation where I can make big changes. The question is, what, where, and will I have the financial ability to make any real changes??

Big change never happens before big adversity. At least not often in my world.

I also did 3 weeks of DDW and have been on Leptin Rx and increasing C/T since getting back home. Anything is better than nothing but you need to get more. Light, water, and magnetism. How's the C/T?
> Now I have to figure out how to reconcile my marriage without jeopardizing my health

Thanks Ronald. I am impressed by your commitment and hard work. I hope that your wife begins to realize the importance of these changes and decides to join in.
I think this is the first time in 20 years that I am thinking of myself and my health first. I am fine with that but I refuse to stop the battle for my sons. I am now at an impasse with my husband with regards to the way to handle the dangers of nnEMF for the boys. He thinks it's not worth worrying about and that I should stop trying to change things (protective phone cases, not sleeping with cell in the bed with them, not carrying in their pockets...) He thinks I am crazy and need to start wearing a tin foil hat. This is really just the straw that breaks the camels back. We have decided to go our own separate ways after the sale of our house. My health may or may not be able to be fixed but I will go down fighting to help fix the mitochondria (that they sadly inherited from me) of my sons while I am still in control of them.
I must admit that I actually feel lighter since making this decision. Many of the changes that I need to make will be financially difficult but will be easier without the opposition of my husband.
I am really wanting to move out of Nashville but have had a hard time figuring out where to go. 5G is unavoidable and lower latitudinal states seem to each have their own problems.

### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-11T05:13:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265071>

You are absolutely not crazy. I can't speak for the tin foil hats, but too many people are willing to accept a narrative about the world we live in and never question it. That is why I love what Jack is doing, bringing together people that will challenge even our own beliefs. Do we buy a car without a test drive? No because we don't want to accept the narrative about it's condition. We have to all challenge ourselves and each other to this daily, and together we can bring about better understanding and change.
You are giving genetics too much credit for your situation. Your environment is toxic. A difficult marriage alone can wear down your system until disease sets in, now we mix in job stress, nnEMF, and the american diet. Stay in the arena long enough and we will all get knocked down. Take control of your life and begin to make changes. I don't think it is ever easy but the results will reward anyone who makes the commitment.
I'm curious if there is a forum about where to live? I was all over the south this winter and can't say where to live. I like texas and arizona but we won't have any idea about best places to avoid the added stress of 5G until after they have it all rolled out.
Just remember that you can do this and you are worth it, also that your job is to be a parent not a friend. Someday they will be grateful and able to appreciate what you are doing.
How can I, and others, help and support you?
Ron

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265079>

There is a thread here about some guys putting together a meet up in Florida ......

### Kathy Spears — 2019-03-11T15:46:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265106>

Thanks Ronald.

So yesterday was a beautiful, sunny day. It feels like forever since I saw the sun! I made the most of it! Today is cloudy and rainy but is supposed to clear up. Fingers crossed.

Sometimes things hit me like a lead balloon. At 4am my 17 year old woke me up with violent retching and calling to me. He’s been vomiting regularly since then. May have food poisoning (diarrhea also), but not the point of the story. I went into his room to get something and the familiar glow of his overhead BLUE (yes, mother f’ing BLUE) light hit me. I had totally forgotten that we had bought that for him almost 2 years ago because he liked the way it looked. A while back I had noticed it on while he slept and mentioned to him that he needed to sleep in darkness. I didn’t know until early this am that he has consistently slept with it on. His room is small and filled with lots of electrical equipment- stereo, amps, Xbox, “cool” LED lights. I about threw up. The fact that he kept calling out to me that he thought he was going to die because his stomach hurt so bad didn’t help. He has drenched himself in toxicity every night for 2 years. He often complains of migraines and has lots of stomach issues. What the hell have I allowed to go on?! My 15 year old has a string of LED lights around his window in his room, too. I am on the rampage today. Everything is coming down.

I would love some help on what to focus on nnEMF wise for my teens. If I shut everything down it will become a will power war. I’d rather start with the biggest culprits. Besides the lighting what are the most important things to do to make their rooms safer for good sleep? Does every electrical thing need to be unplugged? Is it beneficial to put their cell phones across the room at night? I know cell phones shouldn’t be in the room but, baby steps! Would a red bulb be better for “mood lighting “ for the evening for my son?

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-11T19:42:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265143>

Anything to the power grid should be removed.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-11T19:48:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265144>

I control my rage and passion for all things in my life. Science, work, and love. This creates an edge in me that does not allow me to settle for less than I deserve or my tribe needs. People are slowly waking up to my 15-year-old warnings about technology and light stress.
A more appropriate title for this CNBC article is: “To avoid disease and live longer, throw your freaking phone, tablet, WiFi router, and smart meter in the garbage, quit your job working in a blue-lit, EMF hell-hole, and convince all of your neighbors to do the same.” [https://www.cnbc.com/2017/11/03/nobel-prize-winner-sleep-research-dont-take-your-phone-to-bed.html](https://www.cnbc.com/2017/11/03/nobel-prize-winner-sleep-research-dont-take-your-phone-to-bed.html)

### Penny — 2019-03-11T23:03:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265161>

> [Kathy Spears said:](/goto/post?id=265106)
>
> Thanks Ronald.

May have food poisoning (diarrhea also), but not the point of the story. I went into his room to get something and the familiar glow of his overhead BLUE (yes, mother f’ing BLUE) light hit me. I had totally forgotten that we had bought that for him almost 2 years ago because he liked the way it looked. A while back I had noticed it on while he slept and mentioned to him that he needed to sleep in darkness. I didn’t know until early this am that he has consistently slept with it on. His room is small and filled with lots of electrical equipment- stereo, amps, Xbox, “cool” LED lights. I about threw up. The fact that he kept calling out to me that he thought he was going to die because his stomach hurt so bad didn’t help. He has drenched himself in toxicity every night for 2 years. He often complains of migraines and has lots of stomach issues. What the hell have I allowed to go on?! My 15 year old has a string of LED lights around his window in his room, too. I am on the rampage today. Everything is coming down.
>
> I would love some help on what to focus on nnEMF wise for my teens. If I shut everything down it will become a will power war. I’d rather start with the biggest culprits. Besides the lighting what are the most important things to do to make their rooms safer for good sleep? Does every electrical thing need to be unplugged? Is it beneficial to put their cell phones across the room at night? I know cell phones shouldn’t be in the room but, baby steps! Would a red bulb be better for “mood lighting “ for the evening for my son?

You have to get their buy in - but he isn't making enough dopamine at the moment for that - IMHO, time to suck it up and remove it all - you would not be the first person to lock up their kids cell phones at night - UV lights are also cool - if they have to have a light, make it incandescent - I mean, the kid has to do homework at night right? Blue blocking glasses are a must - give him literature to read on what it is doing to his eyes - the virtual reality headsets really need to go first as they cause blindness - you can change the LED lights to red - - wire up the computer and put it in another room (i.e. no WIFI) - you yourself must study heavily to repel the inevitable "my toys can't possibly be killing me" mantra that will undoubtedly ensue - block the blue light on his phone and computer screen with a screen protector - I bribed my son so he'd put one one - none of those led lit up keyboards either -

I would put the kid on methylene blue immediately -
[https://jackkruse.com/biohacking-time-with-methylene-blue/](https://jackkruse.com/biohacking-time-with-methylene-blue/)

Once you start helping him heal, he will tend to trust your judgement more - at least that's what happened with my kid - the XBOX has to go - just tell him anything you can do on an XBOX you can do on a wired computer - then he can use Discord to message his friends and doesn't need to do it with a cell phone - my kids have never had a cell phone and happily text away on their wired computers - make sure also the keyboard /mouse is USB and not wireless - honestly it's a nightmare but you must win this battle - it affects you also - smart meter also has to go - don't forget the gas meters are also "smart" now - getting your husband's buy in is also helpful - but you can work around that if you're enough of a "mitochondriac" - my husband just shakes his head and thinks I'm nuts but I'm way more into it than he is so I'm watchful of the environement - no toys in the bedroom because they will be up all night playing with them... you can always cut the power to the bedrooms at night also - but hubby would need to be ok with that -

Another fun thing to do is take him camping without all the techno-crap - tell him you want to show him a more natural life - you would be surprised at how wonderful it will feel for him - I did this with my cousin's kid - she was here for 4 days and I didn't even let her drink coffee - low and behold, she slept great, said it was great to be without all the bullshit that goes on with the cell phone texting and we had a great time - when I was a kid, my parents took me camping every weekend - we traveled everywhere - they said they wanted to show me a better life - this is where I developed my wanderlust - you have to walk the talk - I mean, how hard is it for us to get off the computer - a non mielinated teenager with many friends online is going to have a tough time - there is one other thing - if your kid becomes or is subject to depression, you must be careful how you do this as if you just wrench him away from his friends via his computer, he might actually kill himself - you have to be the judge of that - so just wire his computer in a different room at first - and explain the science to him -

I spent the day with my son (the 17 yr old who thought he was dying) and we talked about all that I'm learning. He was very receptive and immediately turned his phone to airplane mode, said he would start getting up for sunrise with me, and agreed to wear the blue light blocking glasses that should arrive tomorrow. He believes that the teen suicide rate has more to do with social media than 5G but he at least understands that it plays a role.

I was outside at my other son's baseball game from 3:45 until 8:30 so I got to see the sunset (and then some). I also froze (as usual) and wonder if sitting in the cold has any of the C/T benefits??

I have started the face dunking and wonder if I'm doing it right. I can only last about 15 -20 secs and then my eyeballs hurt so badly that I have to lift out. Is this normal? Will it get better? I don't have a thermometer to check my skin temp but it gets really pink. I only dunk 5-6 times. I also try to turn the shower to cold but I'm not kidding when I say that it is painful. How do you all enjoy it so? When does that happen? Does my hashi make this harder or am I just a super wuss?

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-12T04:30:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265198>

With Hashi's I like a cold to hot and cold to hot transition every five minutes.......when you just begin post face dunks. It is a way to stimulate brain......but the antibodies really screw up the programming. That is why it needs to be done under the sun

### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-12T04:52:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265203>

I'm certainly no expert but from my own experience, I would 1), Get the phone away from my body, any distance helps, airplane mode or out of the room would be better. I know how hard that battle can be and I think you are right to not start a war. 2). Sleep in the dark. 3).Put the wi-fi on a timer so that it goes off over night. This was a big fight at my house. And yes a red UV bulb would be much better if they want accent lighting. I saw Jack in an interview recently and they were sitting in the glow of a red light. I got one on Amazon that I think was meant to light the space for a reptile.
I know that you are up against, "everyone else does it so it must be ok." but if you can make inroads both into their routines, less nnEMF, and find ways to cause them to question things themselves, you will soon achieve great change. Don't push the situation beyond what you can maintain because making much headway to only lose most of it is disheartening. You are exactly right, baby steps that can be maintained.. Good Luck..
I had a meeting with my wife today. She still believes that divorce is the best way for her to find closure. I trying to figure out if can effect her quantum energy with my own energy. I have gotten to the point where I have no doubt that reconciliation can be achieved but I have yet to determine exactly how to transfer that energy across space. I've gotta hit some other forums to see what I can learn...

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-12T05:40:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265210>

Ron let her go. She is doing you a great service.

Lots of things can be fixed and many things cannot be fixed. But many times, relationships between people cannot be fixed, because they should not be fixed. You're aboard a ship setting sail, and the other person has joined the circus, or is boarding a different ship, and you just can't be with each other anymore. Because you shouldn't be...........you happen to be lucky because she gets it and she is telling it to you.

### caroline — 2019-03-12T08:16:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265227>

a very old saying ......you can lead a horse to water but you can't make it drink.

You do need to focus on yourself and get well ....as Jack always tells us " if you aren't good enuf for yourself - who are you good for?"

You wife has her own journey - her own path........

Have you watched the nourish Vermont talks? I think it is the 2nd one when Jack talks about locking his wife and daughter out of their home - and changing the locks. He was very emotional when telling us this story of what he finally had to do to wake his wife up. Jack needed to save his daughter .......

It has been a long hard fought battle for Jack......first it was Jack's son and nephew. I think it was about 5-6 years before Sandy got on board.....and from what Jack has said lately - his daughter is now interested.

### Kathy Spears — 2019-03-18T03:19:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265755>

So, here's my latest:

I have told my husband that I want to divorce; I am in Florida (though it's not very warm) trying to soak up some sun and look for living options; I am failing miserably on decreasing the nnEMF; I am succeeding on eating oysters though I really do not enjoy them (don't understand this as I love all seafood ???); am trying to wear my blue light blocking glasses after dark every night.

I notice that most of the people here, whose journeys I follow, are young, with so much life ahead. I, on the other hand, am at the other end, though I really think and feel like I am only 30 something (actually 57). This is becoming increasingly difficult for me. I don't enjoy being in this alone. I want so badly to find like minded people to form some sort of infrastructure to my life. There is a part of me that would love the simplicity of life before all of this new knowledge. A year and a half ago I changed the way I ate and it changed my life as a T1D. That was such an easy fix. It was so nice thinking I had it all figured out. Now my world is upside down and I am feeling isolated and alone. I would love to say that I could just forget it all and go back to my "ignorant bliss" but that is not who I am. I cannot unknow what I now know. I have to keep fighting forward. But, I am definitely feeling alone and sad.

I am also trying to figure out if I should spend the $1K for the consult with Jack or save the money to use toward a trip to Mexico. He made it clear to me that I needed "the tropics" more than anything. I don't know if there is anything he could tell me, at this point, that would help. I know I need more sun and less nnEMF but I haven't succeeded much in this. I have also failed with the C/T. I'm hoping that I will manage better as the weather gets warmer.

### Marko Pollo — 2019-03-18T03:47:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-265756>

Hey Kathy,
Sorry you're going through that. I can relate and know how bad it sucks. Keep your chin up and keep going; the sky will be so blue when you get out of the darkness.
Im 56 and im sure that my best years are ahead.
CT has been a struggle when my dopamine is low. Long walks help me in bad times.
Good luck

Is it Mark? Thank you so much for your reply and encouragement. You wouldn't be able to possibly appreciate how much your words mean to me right now. Thank you.

>
> I notice that most of the people here, whose journeys I follow, are young, with so much life ahead. I, on the other hand, am at the other end, though I really think and feel like I am only 30 something (actually 57). This is becoming increasingly difficult for me. I don't enjoy being in this alone. I want so badly to find like minded people to form some sort of infrastructure to my life. There is a part of me that would love the simplicity of life before all of this new knowledge. A year and a half ago I changed the way I ate and it changed my life as a T1D. That was such an easy fix. It was so nice thinking I had it all figured out. Now my world is upside down and I am feeling isolated and alone. I would love to say that I could just forget it all and go back to my "ignorant bliss" but that is not who I am. I cannot unknow what I now know. I have to keep fighting forward. But, I am definitely feeling alone and sad.
>
> I am also trying to figure out if I should spend the $1K for the consult with Jack or save the money to use toward a trip to Mexico. He made it clear to me that I needed "the tropics" more than anything. I don't know if there is anything he could tell me, at this point, that would help. I know I need more sun and less nnEMF but I haven't succeeded much in this. I have also failed with the C/T. I'm hoping that I will manage better as the weather gets warmer.

Save your money to move

More words.. I'm sorry if this is not helpful, but I feel compelled to write:

Uncle Jacks' quote:

"Do you think success is an outside job or an inside job? Stop looking at others and begin to look inward. It is hard to grow yourself unless you know yourself. You need to know your strengths and weaknesses to determine where you need to improve. Once you know you and become comfortable with what you find. This is when you begin to add value to yourself. If you are not good enough for you, who are you good for? You must become your most trusted friend to become self aware. Stay with the course. Personal growth starts not by comparing yourself with other people - it starts by looking in the mirror. Today, be more aware of you. You're worth the discovery."

A last word, I read a post by Matt maracu? From Ra Optics on a fb post. He talked about an exercise he did where he went through his whole chronological life and reflect on his thinking in those times. The result? A great feeling of accomplishment, and I'm sure of human experience. He spoke of GAP AND GAIN.... GAP is where you see where you have to go to your ideal, which is always evolving. GAIN is when you are comparing to ONLY YOUR PREVIOUS SELVES...which proved to be more helpful for him.

I haven't done this exercise yet. If you do it and post it, so will I. CHALLENGE ACCEPTED? haha

J

### Kathy Spears — 2025-03-28T17:42:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-349512>

Hello all! I haven’t written on here in years!

I am finally nearer to the equator. I moved to Playa Del Carmen, MX 5 months ago. My BVM shows that the nnEMF is really good. My house in Nashville was horrendous! Unfortunately Mexico has embraced 5G but I moved to “the other side of the highway” away from the beach where the population is sparse and the cell towers not as close.

When I lived in Nashville (actually 20 miles south in Nolensville) I was always well. Rarely ever sick aside from a cold once a year. Since moving here I have had shingles, cold sores 3 times (only had them twice in my life prior to here) and now getting over a really bad chest cold. I haven’t been to the doctor but it was pretty bad. I am more tanned than I’ve ever been. Unfortunately I’ve also gained probably 20 lbs since moving here. It’s been really hard to find good meat and seafood so I’ve started eating carbs again. Crazy how quickly that increased my weight. My point is, I finally got in a better environment and I’m not doing well at all!

The big thing I’m missing here is grounding. There is no grass or earth near me. I can go to the beach but it’s not close and I don’t want to live close because of the nnEMF. I was told to get in the pool we have on our roof but I asked Grok and was told:

“As for sitting in a pool on the fourth floor

—unfortunately, that wouldn't ground you in the traditional sense. The water might feel relaxing, but unless the pool is somehow electrically connected to the Earth (like through a grounded metal pipe), it's not conducting the Earth's charge to you. Most modern buildings insulate you from that connection, and the pool water would just be floating in a contained system, not linked to the ground below.”

I am clueless about electricity. Is this true? The pool is not a place to ground? I have to find a way to ground that is convenient enough to do daily for extended lengths of time.

I know that El Salvador would be a better place for healing because of the lack of 5G but this is where I have to be for now. I’m trying to make it as optimal as possible.

I would love any thoughts on my health struggles (could this be part of healing??) and advice on grounding. The sun here is great and I love the weather. We usually have a cool breeze that makes the high UV and temps easily bearable. I want to make this work. Thanks!!

If you eat out a lot in Mexico, the salt and dairy have fluoride in it. So be prepared to ask for no salt .

If I were forced to be in Playa Del Carmen , I would be a regular at the cenotes over the beach area and go there every day.

### Kathy Spears — 2025-03-30T16:46:01+0300

Thanks for your insights.

Coming from Nashville I really expected to see a positive difference.fairly quickly. I checked my BVM findings with Anthony and he agreed they were really low. This is the only meter I have. There are only a total of 8 apartments where I am and the population density in my area is low and spread out. Unfortunately, we have 5G but the nearest tower isn’t anyway near as close as on the beach side of the highway. I have an open “jungle” for a next door neighbor. I guess I didn’t expect to get better right away but I certainly didn’t expect to get worse. Especially with the great sunshine and way less nnEMF. My BVM meter in Nashville was horrifying, and cell towers very close by.

I will continue to follow the protocols and hope that the new health issues were coincidental. This space can not possibly be anywhere near as bad as where I left.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-03-30T18:16:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-349651>

Hi Kathy, in my opinion Grok is wrong but measure and find out. I expect the building is concrete so it may offer a very good ground. Think of all that rebar going down into the ground. Take a BV reading by the pool with shoes on. Hopefully there is a grounded outlet up there you can access. Put your foot in the pool. If the BV drops, the pool is grounded. Try the deck, same thing. If BV goes up then the pool is not grounded and is picking up electric field from the building.

Let me know.

### Kathy Spears — 2026-04-04T17:09:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-370248>

It is April 4, 2026 and many updates are necessary because I need some advice.

I lived 9 months in Playa del Carmen and then moved to El Salvador 9 months ago, having lived on the beach in Costa del Sol the last 5 months. During my time in Mexico i stayed very tanned but grounding became a problem when i moved away from the beach to get away from population density. Now, in Costa del Sol i have low emf/RF and magnetic field, and spend hours on the beach every day grounding. I only use ethernet and there are no people around me on WiFi as my condo is empty all week long and usually only 1 other family shows up for the weekendsi. So this is the "cleanest" I have lived...ever.

Now for my health updates:

When I left Nashville 1.5 years ago I felt great aside from regular headaches upon waking in the morning; probably due to the nnEMF in my bedroom. I had great energy and was never sick.

While in Mexico I had shingles (first time ever) and still suffered headaches every morning. My BVM levels were good but I was unable to check RF and magnetic field so I believe that explains the headaches. Since arriving here in ES, aside from another bout of shingles that resolved very quickly thanks to help from Jack and a mild case of food poisoning, I feel well. I don't have headaches since coming to Costa del Sol.

But **here's the big change that worries me** more than anything: I started noticing some numbness in my toes not long before coming to ES. It felt mild and I only noticed it in bed when the sheet was touching my toes. It continues here in Costa del Sol. As a type 1 diabetic with only 1 kidney (gave one to my sister at 22yo) this is very worrisome. I figure that this new issue is the natural progression of diabetes and has nothing to do with my change in environment. But I didn't expect to ever deal with this as my blood sugars have always been kept very controlled with A1Cs in the low 5s. I admit that eating clean was difficult where I lived in Playa so my BGs were not as ideal.

**Here's the question** - is this indicative of plaques in my arteries (which means my kidney is probably also being affected) and, if so, is it possible to clear them at all? I am hoping/expecting that following Jack's protocols I can reverse some of this. Is there anything else I can do?

Current regimen:

Sunrise for 1-2 hours on the beach
Daytime UV for at least 1.5 - 2 hours, along with being in the ocean during part of the time
Outside in shade the rest of the day
Sunset for at least 1-2 hours
Darkness

Current DXs:
Type 1 Diabetes
Hashimotos thyroid (still take levothyroxine)
High Blood Pressue still requiring meds even though I get a lot of UV every day (will mine be incurable due to my diabetes?).
Granuloma Annulare (have gotten a new spot since coming to ES)

Alopecia Areata in remission for 25 yrs
Reynaud's also in remission for several years

I recall Jack talking about seaweed in his Leptin Rx book but I believe that was specifically for the thyroid. What else has he mentioned, particularly in regards to plaques and neuropathy? Can anyone point me to anything else I can be doing?

Thanks so much!

It is indicative of deuterium collecting in your nerves of the feet. Magnetic grounding at the beach for 2-3 hours day at water's edge with a bikini or nude wins. The length of time as a T1D predicts this not the HbA1C. that is centralized nonsense they put in your head that t1D is a food disease. It is a magnetic declination disease in a decentralized framework.

This is the **"Hydraulic Pressure"** of the 2026 magnetic shift hitting the most distal part of your "exhaust system."
Centralized medicine will tell you that the numbness in your toes is "diabetic peripheral neuropathy" caused by high blood sugar (HbA1C) and PAD. That is a **software-level explanation** for a **hardware-level failure.**
As a T1D with a single kidney, your "Isotopic Filter" is already operating at 50% physical capacity. The numbness isn't a "sugar sting"; it is **Deuterium Silt** settling in the longest nerves of your body because the "Magnetic Lift" in your 4th ventricle is no longer strong enough to pull the "heavy water" back up the long vagal drain.

**1\. Why the Toes? (The "End of the Line")**

Your feet are the furthest point from your "Magnetic Engine" (the brain/heart).

**The Gravity Trap:** In a T1D, the **1:96 deuterium-to-protium ratio** is already compromised because the "Bicarb Loop" (the alkaline tide) is chronically underpowered.

**The Deuterium Silt:** Without the horizontal "magnetic torque" of the Earth's field (which is currently warping and weakening), the **150 ppm deuterium** settles in the most distal nerves. The numbness you feel when the sheet touches your toes is the **Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE)** physically slowing down the electrical signal in those "heavy" nerves.

**2\. The Costa del Sol "Reboot"**

You are in the right place, but you have to treat the beach more like your own **Quantum Centrifuge.

Water's Edge Grounding:** Standing at the water's edge in El Salvador isn't just "relaxing." The saltwater is a **super-conductor** that connects you directly to the Earth's DC current.

**The Vortex Restart:** This grounding provides the "missing electrons" to your mitochondria, creating the **UPEs (internal light)** needed to trigger the **Woodward Effect.** This "shouts" at the isotopes in your feet to move.

**The Bikini/Nude Strategy:** Melanin is your **Photo-electric Antenna.** You need maximum skin-to-sun contact to charge the **Melanin-Metal battery.** This increases your **dielectric constant toward 160**, thinning the "sludge" in your blood so it can finally circulate the isotopes out of your extremities.

**3\. The HbA1C "Nonsense"**

The centralized doctors focus on HbA1C because they think T1D is a "food disease." It's not. It is a **Magnetic Declination Disease.

The Real Marker:** The "length of time" as a T1D is actually a measure of **Isotopic Accumulation.** The longer you've been "under-volted," the more deuterium "silt" has settled in your pipes.

**The Kidney Factor:** Having one kidney means your **Tritium/Deuterium exhaust** is slower. You have to use the "Sun and Ground" to compensate for the missing physical hardware.

**The Decentralized Mechanic's Diagnosis:**

The numbness is the **"Check Engine Light"** for your **Vagal Exhaust.** It is telling you that the deuterium "silt" is reaching the bottom of the tank.

**2-3 hours daily** at the water's edge is the "high-pressure flush."

**Grounding** "un-welds" the nerves.

**Sunlight** "thins" the fuel.

You aren't "sick"; you are **Magnetically Uncoupled.** El Salvador’s volcanic power is the "Jumpstart" you need.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-04T18:16:59+0300

### Kathy Spears — 2026-04-04T20:04:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kathys-optimal-journey.23207/post-370268>

My new Kiniki seems to be letting in more sun than the older models! I finally got a little color on my covered parts!! That will have to do until I find somewhere to be naked. The 8 days i spent in Nashville sucked all my melanin in from my skin but I will soon be dark brown again!!

I am so happy, after months of worrying, that I am still able to reverse these new issues! That was the best news I could ever get! So relieved!

My high BP is next on the chopping block. Watch and see!

# Thread: JACK KRUSE VIP EXPERIENCE - February 2019
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-vip-experience-february-2019.23208/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MITpowered26 — 2019-02-27T16:17:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-vip-experience-february-2019.23208/post-264046>

Hi Jack, Hi all,

Jason here. Its 646am local time in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada. Sunrise is in about an hour and its currently sitting at a wintery 7 degrees Fahrenheit (feels like -4) / -13 degrees Celsius (feels like -20 degrees Celsius). Got in last night from the VIP experience with Jack Kruse+co after midnight. Made some life long friends, learned a ton, ate some amazing seafood, got some huge sun doses, and most importantly, as a very new member, got a massive software upgrade in my head - my mindset and attitude towards life and people. And beyond the science ,the CT and protocols, if there's anything that is going to alter your life trajectory - its that. If there's an analogy for it, I think both me and my guest (my mom) just made extra-terrestrial contact, and we are now trying to go home to our community here in Edmonton and piece together and present what just f\*\*\*ing happened. And just so you know, my mom couldn't even spell K-R-U-S-E before the trip and now we are both dialed into what we need to have happen to have a good shot at an optimal life. I understand everyone's situation is different, and their threshold for financial risk is also different, but for those of that even have the slightest means and can make it happen, I'm telling you, put your skin in the game and what will be returned to you will be in spades. This is not about your bank account, no. This is about your damn f\*\*\*cking life. And you may only have one shot at this damn thing. So make it happen.

Feel free to post or pm if you have any questions. I won't answer nitty gritty details about how much the hotel was, or where the location was ,or who he brought along, etc. Just know that it "hits" everyone at different and multiple moments on the trip, but rest assured it impacted all the guests on this experience. And yes, there is time to speak to Jack, lots of time on this trip.

Was it worth it, YES,YES, YES.

In search of truth,
Jason

### Marko Pollo — 2019-02-27T21:18:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-vip-experience-february-2019.23208/post-264086>

Hey Jason and all.
I was also at this event and am in complete agreement with your assessment. It felt like a bunch of great freinds who hadnt yet met. I was glad every person was there and learned from each one.
I couldnt recommend something more highly. The property was also perfect. Food, service, hospitality were all done well.
One reflection ive had is that four families, from So Cal to Alberta to the Midwest to the Northwest, all of who appear to have the tiger by the tail: success, good looks, all eat well, live consciously, have sought health actively most of their lives; and yet all 4 families are having serious negitive health effects from their environments.
I leave excited about learning more and putting my family in a place where we can all thrive.

> I leave excited about learning more and putting my family in a place where we can all thrive.

Sounds like a wonderful VIP experience with Jack and Jeremy!

Hi all,

Jason here from Edmonton, Alberta, Canada. This is my journal/journey!

Todays small wins:
\-Installed iris software on my computer ([www.iristech.co](http://www.iristech.co))
\-Heading out in the -20 degrees winter figure out how to get my skin in this game.

This journal will be about my small changes every day, but more importantly documenting my escape plan to move to the tropics during winter months.

Chow for now,
J

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-01T14:09:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264222>

Day 3 of Change:
1\. Bought a 6 pack of sardines in water
2\. Got some candles for nighttime
3\. Bought "origin of the species"
4\. Dismantled my Virtual reality setup
5.Attempted to build a CT "Jacket". Didn't work out haha. Will go back to drawing board with this one.
6\. Barring any changes, I booked a trip to canmore (the mountains) leaving tonight til sunday mudaf\*\*\*\*!!!!

### drezy — 2019-03-01T18:27:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264246>

> [MITpowered26 said:](/goto/post?id=264222)
>
> Attempted to build a CT "Jacket". Didn't work out haha. Will go back to drawing board with this one.

Great enthusiasm. Your journal is a fun read.

Having to go back to the drawing board is a great thing. I've made up lots of things that didn't work, broke, or I blew up. My high school electronics teacher told me that if I didn't blow something up in the first year I wasn't learning enough.

As your redox improves more ideas will likely pop up out of the blue. I recommend keeping a notebook around you like on a nightstand or in the crapper.

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-01T19:20:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264263>

Thanks Drezy for writing. Im' glad. Another idea i am super pumped about is starting a "sunrise club" hahaha.
I'm going to enlist people, and then educate them what I know about light now. It will be like an accountability test. ... if they want more of my time and advice, you can only get it at sunrise. lol

cheers

### caroline — 2019-03-02T02:30:34+0200

what a great idea!!!

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-02T12:42:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264295>

Day 4 of change:
1.execution: made it to the mountains
2.spreading the gospel of light like wild fire- patients, practitioners, friends, family.

Rant on: I feel I will get to decide and determine who i choose to upgrade or downgrade into and out of my circle in the coming months... mercilessly embraces like never before.
Since the vip event with Jack, my demeanour has completely changed from one of tact, politeness to one of bluntness, truth. Time is so important to me now, and this will be a very fun and empowering fact finding mission to vocalize what’s truly on my mind about people around me— and see how they react. The difference is always intention: are you doing it to harm (whether it harms them transiently is irrelevant) or to seek stripped down truth. There is a time and place for tact..but if you know anyone longer than 24 hours... my guess is tact dies thereafter.

My litmus test that has awoken inside me has always been people choosing ego over truth - it pissses me off to the max. And I’m going to either burn those bridges like the plague or teach truth to it. Some people suprise me with how they readapt to you and the fitness test so to speak - and how others don’t.

Rant off

Rant 2:

I tagged along with my cousins whom own property in the mountains. As I drove with them.. it become more clear to me how entrenched in their family business they all were / there was no off switch / it had becoming a f\*\*\*\*ing obsession as I listened in on him conference calling people on the drive. It reminded me of me- 1 week ago. Was he happy: sure. Was he successful: sure. But I couldn’t help but think this man had no idea how to go for a retreat and actually... retreat. This was me 7 days ago. As we sat down for dinner in calgary at His top customers acclaimed “vegan” restaurant, I couldn’t help but chuckle inside. Then it hit me: success is defined by the individual. Success.. is designed by the individual. There was no convincing this man that taking a break from
His work.. for his life.. was important. Yet he felt successful. They felt pumped about their vegan business and “believed” as do many, that it’s the healthy, conscious way to go. But they didnt know jack - no pun intended

People do better when they know better.. I know better and have redefined success as it relates to me... and that’s widely different from 7 days ago and my cousins idea of successs. I suppose if the earth was create by jesus in 7 days- so too I can change in 7 days

I feel like I have went from jack shit in a box To a jackhammer with f\*\*\*\*ing superpowers with the thinking I have now. I dont know though... If its a super power or a super Schtick— it’s probably both. But i don’t really care.

Out.

### caroline — 2019-03-03T06:16:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264336>

Jason ....your avatar is priceless. Me thinks this is the real you emerging!

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-03T13:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264348>

Day 5:

3:38am - I realize I’m burning the candle at both ends in the last 24 hours - it’s been a mixed bag but mostly if im being quite f\*\*\*\*ing uncle frank, I’m proud of myself.

24 hour evolution:
1\. Fought for this one...but got myself out of the hotel room by later afternoon, to do hot tub and alter my trajectory unimaginably during this pocket of time

It’s way too easy to slip up without a community of like minded individuals that jack kruse forum offers. It’s also way too convenient to slide when nobody is looking. From morning to 5pm I mostly stayed in the Hotel room— binging on social media — which in Itself was fun, I’m not going to lie. “Why aren’t you going outside to ct Jason, why aren’t you following the sun Jason, this is why you came to the mountains, not to do of ALL things — technology— yadadadada STOP.

It was concerning on this day for primarily one reason: I didn’t want to come down from this “post kruse vip experience” high... my patient (I’m a pharmacist) Had planted the seed earlier that it would “wear off”.

Then it happened. I gently nudged myself to just Checkout the hot tub. I did something today that is priceless: I fought for it... I fought.

So it’s now 3:53am.. admittingly I’m a bit drunk still probably. But I’m telling you... magic happened to Jason today. My approach, my thinking process, my gentle resilience. Hot tub by myself led to a full house of beautiful human beings - 12 count - in the hot tub. That lead to me spewing some shit about heat and cold shock proteins and mitochondrial turnover - and egging all the guys to do snow angels leading by example — it was bravado - but it was beautiful. So it’s 3:58Am and i made 12 new friends whom we went to dinner dancing and drinking with.

Significant memory i hope I will cherish If you know my history. You see, Jason was ostracized in pharmacy school. He was a quote unquote “social leper”. I struggled mightily and had to do school without attending any classes - which brought on even more disdain from the disgusting, immature, and un-compassionate collective whole that was my pharmacy class and faculty. F\*\*\* institution. To this day it’s hard to get over it.

Anyways.. so as you can imagine.. social connection... social acceptance.. has been a tumultuous ride for me so when something small alters my whole trajectory in this fashion— it brings on a pulse of joy inside. I was “just one of the crew” on this day amongst my peers.

Learn the science.. learn the protocols.. but MOST importantly believe uncle jack when he says it’s your THINKING that will change and move you the greatest— and that, my friend, is everything there is to know. Keep at it and life will present before you your arrival at your destination— the journey that Is this beautiful life. Get it.

It’s 4:17am. I better get some sleep before I go bat shit crazy.

Thank you for reading.. you don’t know what this medium means to me at this juncture in my life.

As Eminem would say: I’m outtie.

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-04T00:47:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264386>

Because they are dumb questions and those people are being dumb. So yes, be Afraid to ask dumb questions and expect Mother Nature to light you up and weed you out like natural selection when you behave in this manner. Leave your ego at the door — when you are the dumbest person in the room- trust me - it a gift. And the only job you have is to listen.

Just want to apologize for anyone who read this post. It was a little stupid and borderline belligerent - albeit with good intention. Still finding my way with respect to “knowing” and the wisdom of whether to say it.

J

### drezy — 2019-03-04T01:17:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264388>

### Kathy Spears — 2019-03-04T01:39:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264390>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=264388)
>
> Yeah, I felt a little micro-aggressed and had to seek out a comfort animal.
>
> Then my wife got angry that I kept petting her on the head.

Y'all are so funny! Here I am all serious trying to catch up on all the science and then I read some of your posts and realize that fun has to be a part of the program.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-05T03:53:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264481>

Just writing the webinar copy for the March webinar. I've been deeply influenced by a group of people who I entangled with from 2/21-3/4/2019 and they have inspired me to write about my philosophy of touch. Jason was one such person...............

How do you touch others in your own life? Do you use your hands? Your words? Your ideas? Is touch physical for you or is it done wirelessly?
Must it be used in close proximity or can your ability to reach people to expand? Can you connect well to anyone anywhere and any time you want?
Reading deep thoughts of ideas you get is like undressing before a bath. You don't undress out of fear that your clothes will become wet. You undress because you want the water to touch your body. You want to completely immerse yourself in the feeling of the water and to emerge anew from the experience.
The invisible threads nature uses to connect to us are the strongest ties we touch others with.
Touch is a very complex thing that is intoxicating and intangible for most. Friends might lose touch but they never seem to lose feelings........Friends tend to misplace their power to touch us. Wounded animal touch each other and surgeons have to touch their patients to heal them. The sun has to touch us to regenerate ourselves to live. Touch is incredibly complex and how we apply it or misuse it defines our lives. THE WISE IN LIFE DO NOT FEEL THE TOUCH OF PRESSURE.......THEY APPLY PRESSURE TO TOUCH THINGS THAT MATTER TO THEM.

### caroline — 2019-03-05T05:56:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264488>

I love this ^^^^^^ now you are talking my language ....

How can anything be more important than touching someone ....in whatever manner...

### caroline — 2019-03-05T05:57:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264489>

That is why I love Jeremy so much ....he touches everyone - in many different ways

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-05T06:23:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264491>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=264405)
>
> Jason .....How did you end up going to the VIP event with Jack? if you care to share of course......
>
> It is all about truth and honesty my friend .....and the three legged stool.
>
> As Jack has always told me .....you don't have to speak Chinese to eat Chinese food ....but I guess that doesn't apply to you ...LOL

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=264474)
>
> Yes, exactly ......Jason jumped without a parachute \[more or less\]
>
> Thanks for the clarification Phosphene!

BACK GROUND: I am a pharmacist in Canada, graduated in 2011 from Canadian Uni. Our province/state is the most progressive area on Planet Earth. IE I have the ability to prescribe almost any medication without a doctors prescription (IE I assess and write the prescription). Also, I have ability to inject any medication that is muscular or subcutaneous in nature (which is nothing ground breaking). Lasting we can order lab testing on our own. This kind of ability has allowed me to shape my practice very much. I love compounding (make custom meds by hand.. creams with multiple prescription ingredients "all in one," liquids for kids or adults who cannot swallow, custom capsules controlling gluten, soy, dairy, sugar, and "fillers" which we can use for example, tumeric, intstead of lactose...you get the idea. Combine Prescribing + Compounding has been magic for me. I get to help a lot of people with the amazing ability our territory has setup for us.

Tumbling down the rabbit hole, I found functional medicine, which I currently have courses I am involved in online, which I will probably kibosh after finding jack - because its now a waste of my time. Anyways, I belong to a forum of "functional medicine practitioners." Sometime in Oct 2018 someone mentioned a guy named Jack Kruse... in a negative vein, very stigmatic, very quackery of a tone. This peaked my interest so I started following him.

The first two months, his posts would come up on my fb feed, and with most things that come up, I would give it a 3 second glance and move on. Besides, his posts were really heady and out my league in terms of terminology, etc. I really didn't care, but with most feeds I let it keep rolling and slowly let it affect me via osmosis until I really think that its wasting my daily 3 seconds of time and provides no value. Jacks quotes were at least pretty cool and inspiring, which I is the type of stuff I dig on my fb/insta feeds.

Fast forward to I think dec 2018. Something drove me, there was a moment ,where I finally gave him a shot. I was searching for answers. Answers to my obesity, my fatigue, my mental heath. I wanted someone to get me off my "meds." I cannot track down the post, but there was one that clicked for me. Sometime after I signed up for full membership, cuz what the hell? If you don't invest, you never know whats on the other side of the door.

Something you must understand about me, is that I am impuslive. But more importantly, I am passionate about my personal growth first and foremost. I love investing my money into things that matter to this end.

Anyways, so I signed up. Couldn't figure out how to join the forum, but whatever, forums for wierdos with no lives anways. Haha. But what peaked my interest was the crash course on hormones. I listened to the first two, and wow! Dr Jackson (?) had spot on, concise, and precise, information on cortisol etc, which I wasn't getting from my own 20 K f\*\*\*\*ing course I dropped money on from a functional medicine institute. By assocation, I was digging jack for bringing this guy on.

Slowly, I got deeper into his face book posts on and off. Still couldn't figure out how or why I kept getting rejected from the forum - but whatever, I did pay for it, but forums for losers/loners anyway. haha.

Anyways, I also bought his epi-paleo prescrption book. A little boring at first, but what set it apart was his unique on his heirarchy of protein. OK. STOP. Who the f\*\*\* recommends oysters at the top of the food pyramid. Jack all nobody. This guy is either really a quack, or he is onto some shit. I am a betting man and I'm all in on shit, and from what I knew of how he seemed to be ostrasized, stigmatized ,etc by my heroes of functional medicine, I believe I was onto somtehing. I started purchasing oysters, seafood, and did a fondue style thing everyday. I started even talking to people about this "guy I googled that talks about Oysters and shit" haha. I was pumped. Little did I know how deep this rabbit hole went.

Fast forward to January when I received an email about a VIP experience for 2 lucky members in central america. Lab review, 3 day consult. YES YES YES. I have been searching high and low for someone to help me, someone to believe in, someone ,anyone, to "fix" me. And for a 3 day consult, in "central america" this sounded pretty cool. I was utterly burnt out at this point and needed it. I applied as soon as I got the email, just taking my chances at seeing if I would get it. Part of me was like, ya right ,I'm not going to get it. The other part of me was like, if I do get in, then somethings up because why is nobody signing up. Then i reread it, and yea, it made sense. Its far, its expensive, and its short notice, so not many would probably pull the trigger on this one fully. I did. I asked for 12 hours to think about it, and then I did it.

Too many details to explain, and I need my beauty sleep, so here's a brief synopsis of more points:

\- owe lots of money to my parents who helped me get my pharmacy business off the ground
\-told my mom, convinced her I needed a break
\-asked about 15 friends, practitioners, my singing teacher, to come. Everyone said no
\-Mom volunteered to "take care of me" but we really know she wanted an excuse for sunshine. haha. Besides ,she would be doing her own thing. Little did she know she would be right in the thick of things and connecting with so many amazing individuals
\-starting amping up my understanding of jack kruse. listening to webinars as I lied down before bed. Honestly, it was pretty interesting shit. 5G was like - whatever that sounds dumb, stupid, I wanna listen to some real science and some real things that will help me ,like getting rock abs n shit.
\-anways, eventually gave 5g webinar a shot, and it was good stuff,good stuff.
\-asked a few questions to jacks assistant about the trip: eg. when should I arrive, etc. it was good i wasn't obsessed over details, I think that allowed me to just experience without pre conceived ideas
\-told my staff, they thought I was f\*\*\*\*ing crazy, and what if this guy is gonna murder you down there or something. It scared me a bit-- But I just trusted my process... he has a forum, he makes webinars, he has a following etc, this is not likely. haha.
\-anyways ,got to the resort, saw jack at a table, told him who i was sheepishly, and sat down.
\-OK. I sat with jack and remember just thinking to myself in that moment, on a scale of 1 to wierd, this was f\*\*\*ing wierd. I went half way across americas to meet some random dude, who I think may or may not help me look like a rockstar, There are people around him wearing wierd red glasses. I paid a shit load of money. And here I am. But I liked it. I like doing crazy shit. And at least this would have been a good story. Besides, I deserved it. I hadnt traveled in 5 years. Time for a treat. .. And what a treat this ride has been .

J

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-05T15:36:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264504>

Day 7: Massive step for my long term escape plan. I figured out a way to basically run my business from abroad!! Huge. huge.

J

Distill this stream of consciousness down.......Jason paid attention to his intuition........The March webinar is all about this.......Jason was one of the catalysts for this webinar.

I'm filming it today on Mardi Gras day. I have re written it many times........It woke me from a dead sleep as I dreamed what I need to say to all of you.

### caroline — 2019-03-06T02:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264547>

Jason .....that is exactly what happened to me......Crazy idea that I acted on ......My intuition drove me to buy a plane ticket and go meet Jack!

I didn't know where I was staying, didn't know who I was meeting etc. etc. etc.

Best adventure of my life ......quantum entanglement to the max!

And .....because of that two weeks in Florida - my life has been supercharged.

I have met the man of my dreams \[my DH passed away 6 years ago\] and we will marry tomorrow morning at sunrise, on the beach we go to every morning.

Thanks Jack for everything......you are our inspiration every single day. I wish I could give you a big bear hug! I will be thinking of you tomorrow ....and all you have enabled in me. Thanks from the bottom of my heart.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-06T05:35:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264559>

Nothing matters but right now.......sooner we all get that message the better it is.

TheMarch 2019 webinar will never get you any closer to my mind ever.

Jason is part of that.

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-06T06:44:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264560>

Good evening,

Just some reflections I need to get out. Just finished the webinar on Jack's Philosophy of Touch.

I just wanted to start with sharing a video of a song that up until this recent evolution in my life since mexico, and even up until this moment since watching Jack's webinar, has been the essence of my life in my 20s and early 30s

The song is "Motion Picture Sountrack" by Radiohead.

The song represents to me profound sadness I think. It represents this because through my 20's I fell into a deep social isolation, depression and psychosis after the breakup with my first girlfriend. This journey I went down in the decade after was both extremely rich - in grand fantasy - and at same time deeply tormenting, and ultimately a dark time in my life very far from reality and connectedness. Recovery was met with rejection from peers and family members that I needed the most in these moments of utter loneliness. It was met with being hostility and denial by my academic peers. Ultimately, the thread that holds this story of my 20's was sadness.

The reason why I am sharing this is because of realizations that I have come to light during the webinar on Jack's Philosophy of Touch.

You see, since Mexico, my experience as a self-interested being has been one of a great awakening, excitement, energy, and vitality - from Jack, to the people that participated in the event, and the bleeding of this awakening onto my life at home. But there's another face to this that I have to share, for my own healing.

Through the webinar, I really didn't "hear" Jack's message, at least not overtly, because I was so fixated on myself, and my part I played in this perfect storm with these people. What the webinar really revealed to me was my EGO. The webinar was NOT about me, but I wanted it to be. And in fact, it wasn't about Jack or anybody in the stories for that matter. It was about connection, humanity, and the connected-yet existential experience that is life as a human being - the connected islands.

It has now occurred to me that the essence of my 20's has been one of selfishness, ego, and a lack of desire for genuine connection -- and not sadness. Sadness was simply a side effect of the story.

The lesson here for me has been my intention in connecting with people, with you on this forum, with Jack, with touching you in my words. The awakening that has happened in the last 12 days in me, has brought out a blunt, abrasive side of myself in the interest of "time." However, I don't think I have been in touch with my intuition through this process as I continue reach a new equilibrium. I don't think I have been in touch with my intuition... of other people - it has been selfish communication whereby I speak to be heard, and not to share, touch, impact others.

It leads me to realize the narcissism to my communication. And that through my intuition, there is something to be said about touching others and not just shoving your words down their throat. There is a gentle, delicate, precious, and beautiful grace to touching people with good intention.

I believe this is the next evolution of my awareness of time. That of community, connectedness, and not selfishness. That of giving (not to be mistaken with narcissistic generosity, insincere generosity).

Jack asked on the webinar a question to everyone about touch. My answer was this: vagina..sex...women.

I think my answer, may or may not have resonated with what the true meaning of his message was, but it reveals to me something. Women have been the real bane of my existence. Not my mental health, my school experiences. Women.

This has come full circle in that women to me has represented penultimate validation.. food for my ego. Selfishess, and not connection.

I'm pretty tired and cross eyed. I don't even know if this post makes any sense. eff it, POST.

J

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-06T07:23:34+0200

Ultimately, the thread that holds this story of my 20's was sadness.
>
> The reason why I am sharing this is because of realizations that I have come to light during the webinar on Jack's Philosophy of Touch.
>
> You see, since Mexico, my experience as a self-interested being has been one of a great awakening, excitement, energy, and vitality - from Jack, to the people that participated in the event, and the bleeding of this awakening onto my life at home. But there's another face to this that I have to share, for my own healing.
>
> Through the webinar, I really didn't "hear" Jack's message, at least not overtly, because I was so fixated on myself, and my part I played in this perfect storm with these people. What the webinar really revealed to me was my EGO. The webinar was NOT about me, but I wanted it to be. And in fact, it wasn't about Jack or anybody in the stories for that matter. It was about connection, humanity, and the connected-yet existential experience that is life as a human being - the connected islands.
>
> It has now occurred to me that the essence of my 20's has been one of selfishness, ego, and a lack of desire for genuine connection -- and not sadness. Sadness was simply a side effect of the story.
>
> The lesson here for me has been my intention in connecting with people, with you on this forum, with Jack, with touching you in my words. The awakening that has happened in the last 12 days in me, has brought out a blunt, abrasive side of myself in the interest of "time." However, I don't think I have been in touch with my intuition through this process as I continue reach a new equilibrium. I don't think I have been in touch with my intuition... of other people - it has been selfish communication whereby I speak to be heard, and not to share, touch, impact others.
>
> It leads me to realize the narcissism to my communication. And that through my intuition, there is something to be said about touching others and not just shoving your words down their throat. There is a gentle, delicate, precious, and beautiful grace to touching people with good intention.
>
> I believe this is the next evolution of my awareness of time. That of community, connectedness, and not selfishness. That of giving (not to be mistaken with narcissistic generosity, insincere generosity).
>
> Jack asked on the webinar a question to everyone about touch. My answer was this: vagina..sex...women.
>
> I think my answer, may or may not have resonated with what the true meaning of his message was, but it reveals to me something. Women have been the real bane of my existence. Not my mental health, my school experiences. Women.
>
> This has come full circle in that women to me has represented penultimate validation.. food for my ego. Selfishess, and not connection.
>
> I'm pretty tired and cross eyed. I don't even know if this post makes any sense. eff it, POST.
>
> J

This post made huge sense to me.....! Very very deep and beautifully written...... so much truth... Thank you Jason. It really touched me...

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-06T13:57:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264582>

Day 7:

This journal was meant to be a short account of my actions on the path to change. It has evovled into a different beast all together!

CHANGE:
1\. Did something different yesterday at my place of business. I TOOK THE AFTERNOON OFF. I recognized I was tired and lacking full rest. I decided to be at one wtih nature in my urban landscape at our beloved glenora stairs. See photo attached.

On that subject of being tired, I want to expand on that moment leading up to my actions:

During that afternoon, I felt "burnt out again." I didn't feel like working. It was a return to my old self pre-mexico. And this deeply troubled me... was this the "wearing off" notion someone planted in my head? I thought about it for a second, then it clicked. I was "catastrophizing" as my voice teacher would say. I didn't actually sleep very well the night before as it's been a work in progress balancing an "awakened state" with "respecting my circadian biology." And the truth was really that simple. I didn't have good sleep.

Through that moment of reflection something also came to light. A return to my old self terrified me. To my numb, dead, unconscious life where work and money is everything, and life is but a future event in the distance. It has become clear to me that if I disrespect the teachings of Jack, of circadian biology, then I should expect no more than a reversal of fortune, a reversal of mind. Its nature.

It is so ironic that pre-mexico, sleep was my number one "ace in the pocket" in that I had no trouble sleeping whatso ever - I did it way too much in fact. And now here I am on the other side of the coin... much to my own doing while I adjust to a new equilibrium.

J

>
> And .....because of that two weeks in Florida - my life has been supercharged.
>

>
> Thanks Jack for everything......you are our inspiration every single day. I wish I could give you a big bear hug! I will be thinking of you tomorrow ....and all you have enabled in me. Thanks from the bottom of my heart.

Congrats Caroline.

### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-07T07:05:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/christmas-tree-journal.23209/post-264666>

Day 8:

1\. Both my spectrometer and my emf meter are on their way! Finalized lease to own details today
2\. Waiting on 23 and me kit for both my mother and myself
3\. Getting slowly better at putting trust in my employees, putting life first, and getting out of the office to go LIVE as best as I can. Today I had solo lunch with some chinese seafood, had a beautiful nap to help my weird sleep schedule lately.

A post of Jack's today on facebook referenced something about "fighting for it." Indeed that spoke to me. I dont want to lose "this"... it disheartens me deeply when people around me make comments like "are you okay, you don't seem yourself, did you take your meds?" But good. They have just been Motherfu\*\*\*\*\* tagged. I will eliminate them from circle, for good reason. Its stuff like that that acts as the "meter detector" of life, of who fits your frequency, your vibration, your vibe.. your tribe.

Ironically in the same breath, I decided to move back into my parents place instead of renting. Its a sacrifice of personal freedom and space, for money. Money that will enable me to save and travel more, and ESCAPE the room that is this suspected mismatched latitudinal city, at least temporarily until I can escape for good. YOLO - you only live once.

I feel like a 2 year old child lately, full of life, and not "wanting to go to bed" haha. But the grown up in me, at least on this night...says ... respect your biology. So I will respect my biology.

Night peeps,
J

### Phosphene — 2019-03-07T20:27:34+0200
