### ElectricUniverse — 2019-05-21T18:40:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wake-up-at-2am.23232/post-270990>

As a seeker of optimal sleep (a fundamental prerequisite to optimal health and longevity) I know there can be times when most of us hit a wall in getting a good night's sleep for a multitude of reasons. An occasional dodgy sleep episode is no cause for alarm, but if it recurs more frequently a sleep deficit could begin to build-- and that is not good.

One significant cause, I believe, (but I've not undergone a medical sleep study) for occasional bouts of bad sleep in past are toxicity (liver, gall bladder, intestines, and other eliminative organs are sweating bullets during sleep to schlepp that sludge around your body to get it ready for excretion).

I've read that some of your body toxin load is continually seeping into intestines 24/7, including during nighttime. Ideally, this goo would be excreted in poop speedily with 2 or 3 bowel movements the next day. Alas, sometimes the toxins just get recirculated in _hepatic portal system_ and so they get another sightseeing tour of the body.

So going on the offensive with a good, ongoing detox program (be sensible about it-- there are many ways to do it wrong and potentially aggravate things) is a good start to improving sleep.

You already know better than to indulge in late day/evening snacking and food indulgences-- that practice will just gum up the eliminative organs during sleep and lead to disturbed sleep (and make you fatter).

You also know that you need to be in an EMF quiet zone to sleep well. If you have Wi-Fi running in your place (or next door neighbor), have a cell phone/smart device near your bed, or are unlucky enough to have cellular towers, smart meters, and antennae nearby then you are being buzzed and zapped during sleep with pulsating radiofrequency waves. Need I say more?

Dr Kruse's prescription for light therapy is key. Besides sun exposure, blocking blue light, and using red light you might want to investigate the use of green light therapy to help with your circadian cycle. It works on non-visual light receptors in brain and could help out (I haven't tried it yet). It is recommended to use true green light bulbs (dichromatic) and not the usual bulbs with a coating of green paint/pigments (or red, etc). As usual, do your due diligence with all hacking.

# Thread: 1,281 days: A journal of recovery from severe ME/CFS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hollyrebecca — 2019-03-04T22:21:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-264465>

After two months of waning energy, strange neurological symptoms, and swollen glands, I finally collapsed on August 29th 2015. I had just graduated. It took me awhile to get a diagnosis, but when I finally did, the consultant said it was the most severe case of ME/CFS he had ever seen. I could not move any of my limbs and I could not speak. I could not comprehend more than three words at a time. I couldn’t tolerate touch and I had continual visual disturbances and a feeling of pressure in my skull. I also had severe light, noise, and movement sensitivity.

I was too ill to research anything, and I didn’t know what to do. I had no help. I lay in a dark room for two and half years. After 2 1/2 years I finally had a little bit of energy to speak. I used this energy to ask my sister to research things. It was a slow process. Around this time I caught a snippet of something on the radio downstairs in my parents house – the chief medical officer saying something about cases of fatigue increasing due to low vitamin D.

My mother had skin cancer when I was a child. I was paranoid about the sun. I wore sunglasses at all times and SPF even in the winter. Before getting ill my immunity was poor and I was particularly prone to urinary and sinus infections. My skin was bad. Both of my parents have auto-immune illnesses. My digestion was terrible. I was on the contraceptive pill for years.

I started taking vitamin D. 10,000iu a day. My muscle strength improved a little bit after a few months. I asked my mother if there was a way I could go outside. She bought me some dark wrap around sunglasses and, for the first time in years, I went into the sun. I stayed out there for about 15 minutes, and when I came in I was so disorientated that I couldn’t walk straight. But gradually, over the course of that summer, I began to stay out for a bit longer.

I hadn’t read anything about ‘primal’ living. I didn’t know it was a movement or anything, but all my instincts at this point were primal. I knew I needed light, sun, company. But it was so difficult because I was so sensitive to these things.

As I became stronger, I started to do my own research. I switched to a paleo diet and started taking key supplements such as, high dose B12, probiotics, vitamin K, and methylfolate, selenium, and iodine. I took Epsom salt baths and sunbathed whenever I could.

Now. I am perhaps 40% recovered. My muscles are stronger and I can cook. I’ve moved to the countryside and my partner grows fresh vegetables. My cognition has improved enough to read and write. I can go out 2 to 3 times a week, and these trips are BLISSFUL! I never thought I would leave my bedroom. I usually go to the woods, or to the beach. My sleep has improved and my mood is good. Asthma and allergies have disappeared.

This month I found this website. I think I got the captcha wrong three times in a row. The information here is dense and I’m still getting my head around it. I have started: grounding, getting AM light, wearing blue blocking glasses, eating seafood, and drinking spring water. In just a month I have noticed that my light sensitivity has decreased.

This illness is a vicious circle. The very things that make us better are difficult for people with CFS to access. By going very slowly I seem to be able to access these things, and I really hope that I can improve more. I’m learning a lot from you guys.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-05T18:26:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-264517>

Best of luck to you. CFS is more widespread than you might think, and it is believed to have many co-factors contributing to morbidity.

There is a large body of research on CFS that has been accumulating over the years. Much of it points to causation by infections/immune dysfunction (mainly the usual suspects-- retroviruses- e.g. EBV and HHV-6, as well as genetically encoded endogenous retroviruses) and a predisposition due to a number of contributing factors, including genetics and environmental exposures. When the terrain is right, diseases come on in and make themselves at home.

Glad you found this forum on your healing journey.

Touch moves us strangely by design.

Is mother nature your mistress? If not, you best engage with her daily. Her game is all that matters in life, death, and love. She is the nurse that heals the infirmed of the mind and body.

The reason life can hurt us so much at times when we are ill is that we have separated our souls from the invisible forces nature wields over us and this forces us into a disconnected existence in life. This discomfort is how she touches you back wireless to give the information that your choices are not wise for you. What matters most is how well you walk through the fire of a challenge, with respect to disease.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-06T21:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-264638>

I have the simplest tastes in life Holly. I am only satisfied with the best for me in this life. I won't settle, period. If you settle for what you've got, you deserve what you get. Is there a difference between giving into something and accepting it? I think so.......

Karma has no menu. You get served what you deserve based upon the choices you touch your life with.

Check the lights you allow around you.......you might be touched by wisdom. Nature always looks back at you.

We always see our worst selves. Our most vulnerable selves when we are infirmed. We need someone else to get close enough to tell us we’re wrong. Someone we trust.........this is why my mistress is nature. Behind her big blue eyes is the touch of a deft surgeon.

We are all wonderful, beautiful wrecks. That's what connects us--that we're all broken, all beautifully imperfect mosaics of stardust and light.

### Hollyrebecca — 2019-03-06T21:52:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-264645>

I think I could come to Mexico. I could go anywhere. There is just enough madness in me (or sanity?). I said I’d do ANYTHING to get better and I meant it.

I’m on the screen too much. I’m isolated here. When people are around I interact and I’m happy, but I’m alone so much. Honestly once I felt grateful for the company of a fly. True story. It felt nice to have another living thing in the room.

I love that we are all mosaics of stardust and light. It’s beautiful and endlessly comforting.

The operation costs £70,000. Even the MRI costs £1,700.

Why would this happen to M.E. patients?

Could cranial instability be fixed any other way?

I’m continuing the Kruse protocol but I am still very ill and wondering if I need to shell out for an MRI.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-06-25T16:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-273256>

low solar redox = poor ligament and tendon and disc function.

Improve your ability to think = low solar redox too

-   -   Thinking is not IQ. When people talk about thinking they make the mistake of thinking that people with high IQs think better. That’s not what I’m talking about. I hate to break it to you but unless you’re trying to get into Mensa, IQ tests don’t matter as much as we think they do. After a certain point, that’s not the type of knowledge or brainpower that makes you better at life, happier, or more successful. It’s a measure sure, but a relatively useless one.

        If you want to outsmart people who are smarter than you, temperament and life-long learning are more important than IQ. Become a life long learner and do it under the power of the sun and the absence of the sun during night. Nothing works better or is as simple for the functioning of the human brain says this brain surgeon.

        Two of the guiding principles that Black Swans should follow on their path towards seeking wisdom are: (1) See the sunrise every AM and go to bed smarter than when you woke up; and (2) I’m not smart enough to figure everything out myself, so I want to ‘master the best of what "parts" of the truth other people have already figured out.’

        Acquiring wisdom is hard work. Learning how to think is tougher. It means sifting through the information, filtering the bull shit, and connecting it to a framework built around Nature's wisdom that you can use. A lot of people want to get their opinions from someone else who is selling a product or narrative. I know this because whenever anyone blurts out an opinion on social media I know is suspect and I ask why, I get some hastily re-phrased sound-byte that doesn’t contextualize the problem, identify the forces at play, demonstrate differences or similarities with previous situations, consider base rates, or … anything else that would demonstrate some level of thinking. One of my favorite questions to probe thinking is to ask what information would cause someone to change their mind. Immediately stop listening and leave if they say ‘I can’t think of anything.’ That is how I operate my circle of six. I want humans who know how to pack their own parachutes because they think well.

### Woodstock — 2024-10-10T07:45:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-340743>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=264610)
>
> I have watched a video or two by Dr Sarah Myhill on CFS (they call it ME in UK). She is a smart doctor and worth listening to.
>
> Another expert's research I follow is Dr Dietrich Klinghardt. His specialty is Lyme and Autism but his expertise runs gamut of human infectious disease. Like Dr Kruse, he has been sounding alarm on RF/MW radiation for many years.

I follow Dr. Klinghardt too. I was searching for the answers to my chronic pain about 10 years ago when I came across a lecture he did on retroviruses. So I started searching for information on retroviruses and I came across a lecture on SV40 being the cause of cancer and learned of the book Dr. Mary’s Monkey which I read immediately and listened to every interview I could find with Ed Haslam the author. At the time I found Dr. Klinghardt I didn’t think I had Lyme disease because I have had symptoms since early childhood and had been diagnosed with Fibromyalgia in my 20’s. I had one year of remission and then it was another 25 years before finally figuring out the Lyme part. About 5 years ago I was having a lot of neurological issues until I got rid of Wi-Fi and wired my house with Ethernet. I listen to Dr Klinghardt’s weekly talk. I went to his Seattle clinic to get diagnosed. But his detox protocols were too intense for me and I felt worse. Also I didn’t have the mold part figured out. We did a lot of remodeling so I didn’t think there was mold in my house but it was in the crawl space and under the bathroom floor in some rotted out toilet water pipe that was dripping. Only when we ripped out the tile floor and looked underneath did we find it. I had felt a very strong pull to go to the ocean. I went to Hawaii and the Florida 9 months ago and felt so much better in both places despite the humidity. I’m not much of a morning person so it wasn’t until last week when I came across Dr. Jack that I am finally getting this piece about seeing the sun rise every day! I saw Jack on the Danny Jones podcast. I had given up on Danny Jones as controlled opposition but I saw a clip on Twitter of the podcast and since I already knew about SV40 (but can never seem to get anyone to believe me about this), I was thrilled to find Jack who was well versed on it and adding some pieces that weren’t in the Dr. Mary’s Monkey book. Also I am a huge RFKjr follower. I am even more so excited about RFK since he stopped pushing the false climate change narrative. I still have a lot to learn here but I AM HOME! You all are my people. Now I just have to figure out how to get to the beach.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-10-13T19:28:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1-281-days-a-journal-of-recovery-from-severe-me-cfs.23239/post-340935>

**DID YOU KNOW YOUR GOVERNMENT CREATED LYME/MOLD EPIDEMIC VIA MKULTRA/BRAIN HEALTH INITIATIVE?**

Jack can you explain what is going on with ME/CFS? Fibromyalgia? I saw in the comments on your forum that you say its a tough fix but its possible. I'm sure it has to do with water chemistry and dysfunction of the autonomic nervous system and the gut. Because it causes such temperature dysregulation, is Cold Thermogenesis and sunlight something still to consider to regenerate the DC current and restore the flow of electrons? Thanks Jack!

ANSWER: Nature had the foresight to make decisions in hindsight using sunlight. Why? Because light has more non linear properties than anything in the cosmos. The government found out how to make LIGHT A BIOWEAPON in the 1950s. It should be both fascinating and humbling to scientists that the massless, timeless, and phenomenal manifestation of light are the irrefutable requirement for all physical structure on Earth and in the cosmos. It turns out these abilities are why ME is mis-understood. The only constant in a universe of relentless flux because of how light works in us.

The problem with these "discoveries" is that they become systematized and programmed by paradigms. The public needs to realize that "systems and programs" are for computers.... and they are not designed for living things like us. We are designed to be wirelessly connected to the sun and fully connected to Earth to work properly in nature to remain well.

We simply need to connect with nature and feel the zeitgebers..........and Mother Nature will take care of us.

My answer is complicated and illuminating: If you read the last few blogs in my OSF series of blogs at [www.jackkruse.com](https://forum.jackkruse.com/www.jackkruse.com) you will see the reason a system goes south is because there is not enough electrons present within the cell membranes or the mitochondria of that system. Fibromyalgia is a loss of electrons in the muscles and collagen fascia of the entire body that dehydrates us (mitochondria) and lowers our piezoelectric current. This lowers the dielectric constant in cell water and it is become magnified when things like blue light and nnEMF dehydrate our cells. It usually starts in one cell type but it can spread fast if your base line redox is low. It also will alter proton recycling in the mitochondrial matrix. So if you are "young healthy 20 something" who works out in a blue lit gym, or a blue lit Harvard lab, you by definition, have a lowered redox potential because you DESTROYED POMC signaling via alpha-MSH. You also live at a high latitude and in a city with high population density when you were struck down. You likely have no idea how those two things dehydrate cells to set the stage for ME/FM/CFS. Dehydration of cells due to mitochondrial damage is the key initial starting point for ME. It can lead to a local loss in physiologic control over certain muscles too, just by a lack of red light from the skin above the muscles. The goal is not to avoid foods, molds, or toxins, with this issue, but to fix the structure of the tissues and gut by replacing the electrons lost to build the capacitor in cell water back. This turns your energy engine, the mitochondria, back on to protect your cells once again. Here is how I figured it all out.

Read and share it if you find it illuminating.

# Thread: Jakes Optimal Journal

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal

### jake lalich — 2019-03-05T05:14:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jakes-optimal-journal.23241/post-264485>

Hi I am Jake,

absolutely new to this forum, thank you for having me.

33 years old living in Perth Western Australia, I was recently told about Dr Kruse's work thru a friend and have spent countless hours reading and listening to the information he makes available. I have signed up to the platinum membership and looking forward to diving into all the material.

I have been managing symptoms of Ankylosing Spondylitis thru lifestyle modification since I was around 26 years of age. whilst spending some time living in Las Vegas for training a couple of years ago I had my worst flair up of my condition. I returned to Perth and have gotten somewhat of my mobility and reduced pain but am still having to take the occasional anti inflammatory to be able to get up and go to work, I live a fairly active life and am a coach in a movement facility. I have been a student of Ido portal for around 4 years and found that movement has been my best medicine (so far) im looking to optimise every area of life I can and reverse the "condition".

I've recently started getting out in the morning sun a couple of hours a day usually between 7-10am
started eating a lot more seafood (rarely ate it before) now id eat oysters especially 2x week
I do cold bath 1 x per week 20 min at 12 degrees Celsius and looking to do this more often as im finding a lot of benefit from it

I run movement retreats in Bali Indonesia and these trips are always therapeutic for me. looking to run more and more of them each year

it is only until recently and thru jacks work I am able to see areas I need to address that were never even a thought for me prior. thank you

I am out to reverse ankylosing spondylitis and I believe I can with the help and implementation of this incredible information. for a while now I've come to accept the flair ups, the pain and discomfort that I live with daily but today im no longer willing to accept less than optimal.

### jake lalich — 2019-03-23T07:39:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jakes-optimal-journal.23241/post-266258>

So the more I read, listen to podcasts with Jack the more things like this start smacking me in the face. This is the facility I currently work out of. working towards a more optimal environment to teach from.

I have gotten well into the routine of waking up at sunrise eating within 30 minutes of waking then riding my bike down to the beach and jumping in the ocean for 20 minutes or more. following that ill go home and lay out in the sun to dry off and read whatever book im reading at the moment. currently the movnat book that Caroline suggested to me.

I train a little outdoors and then lunchtime I train Brazilian jiu jitsu at my martial arts school.

usually ill start work at around 2pm and finish by 7 sometimes 8 pm.

I've switched to predominantly eating seafood and reduce my intake of carbohydrates. I was drinking kombucha regularly but have scrapped this.

im doing cold thermogenesis in a tub 50-55 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 min 3 -4 nights a week post dinner and before bed which is helping me sleep a lot.

still so much to work on. so much to learn so much to apply. its playing a long term game and making changes as fast as possible.

im feeling positive. its early days

### jake lalich — 2019-03-27T13:41:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jakes-optimal-journal.23241/post-266561>

so I've been a platinum member with [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) about 20 days now. I wanted to recap for myself how far ive come already as its easy to always see the next goal or outcome and not stop and reflect

since then I have

not missed a sunrise and actually started waking up around this time before my alarm goes off on a few occasions

ride my bike to the beach and jump in for 10-20 min
have actually got my girlfriend doing this with me which is great as she is a Dr and working in the hospital blue light and night shifts on occasions.. ive noticed a much better mood from her when she does night shift but jumps in the ocean after coming home.

cold thermogenesis 3-5 ays per week

re arranged my work schedule to allow more time in AM sunlight I spend about 45 min each morning reading in the sun

wearing ra optics glasses at night and when working from my computer.

eating a lot more seafood

listened to Gerald pollocks podcast on the rewild podcast. had to listen twice blew my mind

ordered the book the body electric which is on its way to me in the mail

feeling upbeat and noticing more energy without needing to rely heavily on caffeine

I have been listening to all the recent podcasts webinars and Q and A

I've taken the not wasting my time completely on board and looked in every area of my life that I'm wasting time.

made quite some changes in the last 2 days in regards to my work and the direction I want to take it which is completely on a different path tHan what I was on but is what is true to the dream I have for my life.

### jake lalich — 2019-05-21T16:32:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jakes-optimal-journal.23241/post-270979>

Have not posted here in a while. I recently had someone come around to test my home and was quite surprised by a few findings. one of the things I have done since is turn the power off to the house from the circuit board at night when going to sleep as the electric fields at the head of the bed coming from the wall was over 1000mv. ive started waking up feeling rested for the first time in a long time and don't find myself getting to pee in the middle of the night anymore

# Thread: New Glacier forming around the 45 degree latitude?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-glacier-forming-around-the-45-degree-latitude.23246/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Allin — 2019-03-06T03:58:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-glacier-forming-around-the-45-degree-latitude.23246/post-264555>

I recently heard a new version of how our sun travels through the galaxy in more of a comet like configuration with the planets following in a rotational or corkscrew pattern behind. The sun which now has a lower temperature and a smaller corona because of the "denser"medium in which the sun is now traveling through, more stuff such as cosmic radiation is not now being blocked by the sun like it did before. You could say more _s_tuff like cosmic radiation is getting around the sun to us causing more clouds to form which have been making the planet cooler but I am thinking ... might it be more nnEMF which we are creating is not being able to get out?

With the recent information (for me) that the Van Allen radiation belts now number 3 instead of 2 which was caused by our use of VHF, UHF, radar, (nnEMF) etc since 1940, a more likely a combination of the loop or coupling of the is increase cosmic radiation in and more nnEMF can not get out?

Now as part of the "coming" Ice Age, along the 45 N degree latitude, not this summer but next summer, where it is snowing in June, July and August will be at the center of the next glacier.

Also I heard the next Ice Age will come to the southern hemisphere with the same prediction of snow during summer but not any location or if in AU or NZ.

Thoughts?

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-06T18:40:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-glacier-forming-around-the-45-degree-latitude.23246/post-264607>

> [Allin said:](/goto/post?id=264555)
>
> I recently heard a new version of how our sun travels through the galaxy in more of a comet like configuration with the planets following in a rotational or corkscrew pattern behind. The sun which now has a lower temperature and a smaller corona because of the "denser"medium in which the sun is now traveling through, more stuff such as cosmic radiation is not now being blocked by the sun like it did before. You could say more _s_tuff like cosmic radiation is getting around the sun to us causing more clouds to form which have been making the planet cooler but I am thinking ... might it be more nnEMF which we are creating is not being able to get out?
>
> With the recent information (for me) that the Van Allen radiation belts now number 3 instead of 2 which was caused by our use of VHF, UHF, radar, (nnEMF) etc since 1940, a more likely a combination of the loop or coupling of the is increase cosmic radiation in and more nnEMF can not get out?
>
> Now as part of the "coming" Ice Age, along the 45 N degree latitude, not this summer but next summer, where it is snowing in June, July and August will be at the center of the next glacier.
>
> Also I heard the next Ice Age will come to the southern hemisphere with the same prediction of snow during summer but not any location or if in AU or NZ.
>
> Thoughts?

That is a good synopsis of current fact-based thinking on climate change--that is, outside the crazy echo chamber of global warming alarmists and their high priests like Al Gore. Climate change is here but it is not warming caused by humans and CO2.

Quite to the contrary: the sun is main driver of earth's climate and always has been. It is only that dodgy human trait called hubris and conceit that would cause people to conceive that human activities alone are end all and be all of earth changes.

Like all of nature, our Sun is driven by mysterious but nonetheless real and unending cycles. Those of us alive today have the unhappy distinction of being around at a very inauspicious time in many ways.

**We like to think of Sun as only our benign friend that nourishes and heals us.** Au contraire, in reality he can be capricious and very destructive to his charges in solar system. The geologic record and modern research illustrate this chilling point time and again.

Our current severe cold/snowy winter in Northern Hemisphere is just the opening salvo in Grand Solar Minimum cycle that is coming in. Many experts in this field are expecting intensification for next few years, and a true mini Ice Age within a decade. If our new ice age emulates the Maunder Minimum epoch or worse then we could be looking at a 40-400 year or longer glacial period (no one knows for sure).

It is correct to say Sun's (and earth's) magnetospheres are weakening and providing much less protection from solar wind and cosmic rays. This add-on effect will cause more cloud cover and other deleterious results that will naturally intensify the next ice age.

We need to prepare as a society for a long dark winter (figuratively) and toss all this pseudo scientific hooey about global warming in dust bin. Greatest threat will be mass starvation caused by crop losses from shorter growing seasons and erratic weather phenomenon (e.g hail, etc) that destroy crops in fields.

Strap in tight folks, it will be quite a sleigh ride.

>
> **We like to think of Sun as only our benign friend that nourishes and heals us.** Au contraire, in reality he can be capricious and very destructive to his charges in solar system. The geologic record and modern research illustrate this chilling point time and again.

Many experts in this field are expecting intensification for next few years, and a true mini Ice Age within a decade. # Thread: HAVE YOU EVER WRITTEN A LOVE LETTER TO YOURSELF?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-written-a-love-letter-to-yourself.23249/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 9

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-06T18:05:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-written-a-love-letter-to-yourself.23249/post-264599>

The philosophy of touch is different for us all. You have to find yours. We can touch each other physically or mentally using overt and covert ways. Have you Do not focus on one option over another. Explore them all. I just did and it cut me and I bleed. Now I am letting you see me gutted.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Bleeding isn't optional for most of us, but maybe it should be? Not all wounds we create or bear are the bleeding kind but if they touch others they might be worthwhile.
Sometimes, reaching out and taking someone's hand is the beginning of a journey. At other times, it is allowing another to take yours.
Sometimes this journey begins when the touch is delivered wirelessly by an idea or a smile, or a word. No matter the mode, engage them with your power. Get so inside their head that they cannot help but feel your power.
Just as a life without love is incongruent, so is a life lived without the ability to touch. I am of the opinion it marks the genesis of a serious illness......it might be an illness from which you never entirely recover.
Tell your story.
Shout it. Write it.
Whisper it if you have to.
But tell it to the world.......
Some won't understand it.
Some will outright reject it.
But many will thank you for it.
And then the most
magical thing will happen in your life.
One by one, voices will start
whispering, 'Me, too.'
And your tribe will gather.
And you will never feel alone again.
With" a touch" of wisdom, can emerge the end of fears & disease.
The nose on your face is genetic. Intuition is epigenetic. That means it is wholly impacted by our environment. Our intuition is NOT always right but you can hone it to become the greatest tool in your epigenetic toolbox.
Its power and range are unmatched in nature.
Refuse to be ignored. Touch the masses in the ways that empower you. The soul that lives inside this body will not be ignored. I am here to stay and I am going to make you feel me in some way with or without your consent.
This is the story of the March webinar of 2019. It is quite a ride. It is a story of love that crashes into us via touch and when you are touched in this way you might gain some wisdom. Listen...........it might just change your life.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/25180098](https://www.patreon.com/posts/25180098)

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-07T14:11:45+0200

### Hollyrebecca — 2019-03-07T19:08:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-written-a-love-letter-to-yourself.23249/post-264716>

This post reminded me of this Mary Oliver poem - ‘Wild Geese’. Reading this feels like an act of self-love, somehow:

‘You do not have to be good.
You do not have to walk on your knees
For a hundred miles through the desert, repenting.
You only have to let the soft animal of your body
love what it loves.
Tell me about despair, yours, and I will tell you mine.
Meanwhile the world goes on.
Meanwhile the sun and the clear pebbles of the rain
are moving across the landscapes,
over the prairies and the deep trees,
the mountains and the rivers.
Meanwhile the wild geese, high in the clean blue air,
are heading home again.
Whoever you are, no matter how lonely,
the world offers itself to your imagination,
calls to you like the wild geese, harsh and exciting —
over and over announcing your place
in the family of things.’

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-06T18:45:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-been-touched-by-cold-how-did-it-feel.23250/post-264608>

Chapters in medical textbooks have been re-written because of how CT works by touching our ANS.
Humans have an outstanding ability to keep themselves warm in cold weather or ice-cold water.
The autonomous nervous system is partially voluntary.
This means we need to train to withstand the cold like you would lift weights........let the cold touch you to regain your magnetic flux.

[https://www.wimhofmethod.com/uploads/kcfinder/files/biology-now-chapter-22-Wim-Hof.pdf](https://www.wimhofmethod.com/uploads/kcfinder/files/biology-now-chapter-22-Wim-Hof.pdf)

JT has a lot to say about Cold Thermogenesis touches him.
"Once you explore this world of Citizen Scientists and Quantum Biology youʼll no doubt come across the term BioHacking. The idea is to experiment or hack your environment to try and decide which variables are meaningful. You have done it without knowing you were doing it perhaps with a diet or workout routine- You've mitohacked your environment.

Thereʼre stresses we consider inherent to our job or family that can be mitigated with actions like Cold Thermogenesis. You might work the night shift or have trouble sleeping- if you do have a light problem it will be hard for someone else to fix it for you. No matter the good advice or the books you read you need to tune your body so it craves good things. That is my challenge to myself: I want to tune my intuition so that I no longer only crave sex and gummy worms but instead desire cold water and sunshine. That was my goal when I first began, it feels silly now to admit because of my sugar addiction but that is where my CT rituals all started for me. My goal is still the same; to crave goodness.

We have talked about anticipating touch from others, this is your opportunity to do what you can to clean your slate and be a vibrationally responsible human who is spreading goodness and thinking clearly. How does CT pertain to touch?

_**Ask yourself when is the best time to sharpen a knife?**_ The answer is before you need it, you may not even know you needed it. This is me telling you: no matter your discipline, no matter your fitness level, you will heighten your senses and sharpen your mind when you practice Cold Thermogenesis. This is a promise from nature. The cold will increase your magnetism in ways you'll never anticipate. You will attract more and should, therefore, better understand the consequences of gravity."

CT builds your animal magnetism.

### kris90 — 2019-04-12T20:33:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-been-touched-by-cold-how-did-it-feel.23250/post-267933>

I became quite curious in the past couple days of why cold REALLY works. Here are some observations that are leading me down a rabitt hole:

\-CT series taught us that cold activates the leptin-melanocortin pathway by making POMC when cold touches our skin
\-POMC is also made from UV light touching our skin, and it makes b-endorphin, ACTH, a-MSH and a few other chemicals
\-I thought it was interesting that both cold and UV light activate this same pathway. How can that be so?
\-I learned from Jack that cooling of surfaces allows more light assimilation
\-I also learned from Jack that all cells release ELF UV light for signalling, and stressed cells release more (i.e. loss of energy from light)
\-I also learned from Jack that melanin is a protein that absorbs and stores UV light (it has photon traps) and it is on the skin and RPE
\-In winter, there is barely any UV light in our environment, but somehow we are designed to thrive. Cold plays a role. But how does it work?
\-UV light does not ALWAYS have to come from the SUN. Jack taught me in Vermont 2018 it comes from within, and hence why cells release ELF UV light.
\-Cold on the skin MUST help re-absorb the ELF UV light released/lossed by stressed cells. Perhaps melanin doesn't always absorb UV light from the sun, but also reabsorbs UV light emitted by cells?
\-Assuming this is correct, the ELF UV light from our cells that gets recaptured in our skin is the mechanism for activating the leptin-melanocortin pathway and making POMC.
\-Again, if true, cold simply PREVENTS a loss of energy from light keeping E=mc2 balanced.

So my takehome is this:
\-If you live in the Northern Hemisphere, you are designed to harvest as MUCH UV light from the sun as possible in summer months to build your solar redox, store UV light in melanin and increase your Vitamin D levels.
\-In winter, the cold on your surfaces limits the amount of ELF UV light biophotons that ESCAPE your body, so you can RE-USE light energy. Hence why cold increases your magnetism, so you are a better light collector and carrier, and you are less leaky.

The fastest way to reverse disease is to better assimilate light. This is why equatorial sun is a MUST for those who are quite ill losing too much light from their cells. You MUST build your redox first. THEN, you can use COLD to minimize your leakiness of light. Keeping light within the body is KEY to maintaining optimal health and wellness.

### kris90 — 2019-04-17T21:59:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-been-touched-by-cold-how-did-it-feel.23250/post-268307>

>
> Kris, this summary is much appreciated especially for myself as I spend an inordinate amount of time (years) trying to connect these dots and figure out my own puzzle. Any thoughts on how this connects to CT's ability to improve various receptors sensitivity throughout the body? For neurotransmitters and hormones etc? I'm one of the odd ducks that has too much dopamine and norepinephrine etc. (COMT, anxiety blah blah etc). Initially, CT increased anxiety then oddly is providing a balancing effect over time. The effect of CT is turbo charged when combined with sunlight or redlight which supports your redox first note above. Glad it was helpful! My take is that cold condenses our proteins, while light and/or stress uncondenses our proteins. Remember how Jack talks about the geometry of our proteins is critical? Well in a condensed state, the receptors are smaller, and less active hormone is required to activate them. Additionally, hormones have to travel and bind to the receptor. To me, that is driven by a force of magnetism, and given that the cold improves magnetic flux, it keeps hormones bound to receptors a lot tighter.

### kris90 — 2019-04-18T15:30:28+0300

> My take is that cold condenses our proteins, while light and/or stress uncondenses our proteins. Remember how Jack talks about the geometry of our proteins is critical? Well in a condensed state, the receptors are smaller, and less active hormone is required to activate them. Additionally, hormones have to travel and bind to the receptor. To me, that is driven by a force of magnetism, and given that the cold improves magnetic flux, it keeps hormones bound to receptors a lot tighter.

Further to this, when we are full-blown cold adapted and pretty well lost all our fat mass (as mammals do throughout hibernation), we are in our most condensed state, which means we are not going to release very much hormone (think VERY low leptin levels) into the blood. Instead, the hormone stays bound to the receptor very tightly. This is why IME, my LH and T levels are quite low, and I am very lean. There's not very much T in my blood, but I am not experiencing hypogonadism. I have a normal libido, sperm count, can grow a great beard, and have quite a bit of muscle mass with minimal fat.

On the other end of the equation, someone may have leptin resistance and obesity with low T levels. The opposite is occurring. The individual is too UNcondensed (think fall when mammals went through an entire summer eating carbs) and therefore TOO much hormone (energy/information) is being released/lost.

### kris90 — 2019-05-01T16:17:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-been-touched-by-cold-how-did-it-feel.23250/post-269402>

I noticed something quite remarkable. My skin color changes in the cold. Where I live is still quite cold in the mornings, so when I ground and watch the sunrise in my underwear, I'm getting CT in as well. Then I usually do some CT in the shower or pool, and on my way into the office I drive with the windows down for air CT and at this point I'm shivering like crazy.

I noticed my arms get really dark (a nice bronze), even on cloudy days, just from the cold alone. I think the CT is driving up my a-MSH through POMC to give me this complexion.

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=267933)
>
> I became quite curious in the past couple days of why cold REALLY works. Here are some observations that are leading me down a rabitt hole:
>
> \-CT series taught us that cold activates the leptin-melanocortin pathway by making POMC when cold touches our skin
> \-POMC is also made from UV light touching our skin, and it makes b-endorphin, ACTH, a-MSH and a few other chemicals
> \-I thought it was interesting that both cold and UV light activate this same pathway. How can that be so?
> \-I learned from Jack that cooling of surfaces allows more light assimilation
> \-I also learned from Jack that all cells release ELF UV light for signalling, and stressed cells release more (i.e. loss of energy from light)
> \-I also learned from Jack that melanin is a protein that absorbs and stores UV light (it has photon traps) and it is on the skin and RPE
> \-In winter, there is barely any UV light in our environment, but somehow we are designed to thrive. Cold plays a role. But how does it work?
> \-UV light does not ALWAYS have to come from the SUN. Jack taught me in Vermont 2018 it comes from within, and hence why cells release ELF UV light.
> \-Cold on the skin MUST help re-absorb the ELF UV light released/lossed by stressed cells. Perhaps melanin doesn't always absorb UV light from the sun, but also reabsorbs UV light emitted by cells?
> \-Assuming this is correct, the ELF UV light from our cells that gets recaptured in our skin is the mechanism for activating the leptin-melanocortin pathway and making POMC.
> \-Again, if true, cold simply PREVENTS a loss of energy from light keeping E=mc2 balanced.
>
> So my takehome is this:
> \-If you live in the Northern Hemisphere, you are designed to harvest as MUCH UV light from the sun as possible in summer months to build your solar redox, store UV light in melanin and increase your Vitamin D levels.
> \-In winter, the cold on your surfaces limits the amount of ELF UV light biophotons that ESCAPE your body, so you can RE-USE light energy. Hence why cold increases your magnetism, so you are a better light collector and carrier, and you are less leaky.
>
> The fastest way to reverse disease is to better assimilate light. This is why equatorial sun is a MUST for those who are quite ill losing too much light from their cells. You MUST build your redox first. THEN, you can use COLD to minimize your leakiness of light. Keeping light within the body is KEY to maintaining optimal health and wellness.

Excepost, I particularly like what you say about storing up lightin summer to last over winter, this makes sense I totally resonate with that

>
> "Cold" is a longing. Being in the "cold" and not feeling that it compromises ones survival is power.

As a strange side note from the weird facts department, it is well known that people in cold suffering from severe hypothermia are known to strip off their clothes (technically this is called 'paradoxical undressing' and is related to near a terminal hypothermia situation when survival mechanism of vasoconstriction fails and a rush of blood from body core to extremities causes a sensation of heating up).

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-12-30T18:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/have-you-ever-been-touched-by-cold-how-did-it-feel.23250/post-284063>

> [Alyce Miles OD said:](/goto/post?id=283512)
>
> Yes....Last year I shivered and was so cold in the showers I took. When the water got down to 50 degrees I was like...."I can do this" Repeat to myself to reinforce it!!!
>
> Fast foreward to this year.... a cold shower feels better than a warm one. And I am wondering if the water will get any colder.......
>
> My kid has the flu this week and I am feeling excellent. I am the only one embracing cold in the home.

The [**Ice Maiden**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OAuNdpxnm7k&t=1207s) who lives in Russia has taken cold thermogenesis to a new level, apparently.

We know how cold it can get in Russia, but this unusual lady walks around in summer clothes while everyone else wears heavy winter clothing. She claims the cold doesn't bother her and makes her healthy and younger. She is a member of the 'long lifer club' there.

In my opinion, I suspect our cold climate ancestors (Neanderthal, Cro Magon) endured cold environments in similar ways until we got civilized and lost our natural extreme cold adaptation.

**Today as modern members of advanced societies we value creature comforts** and demand a steady, ideally comfortable indoor environment all year round.

Many of us go from air conditioned (heated or cooled according to season) house to car to work or other activities and back home without necessarily being exposed to outside conditions in a meaningful way.

It is my theory that to be optimally healthy we need to be exposed to our natural climate conditions where we live. In winter that means being exposed often to cold conditions and in summer being subjected frequently to heat and humidity (in temperate climates).

### halvibe — 2020-01-04T23:58:41+0200

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=283942)
>
> I'm sure not buying any life insurance policy on you for that.

Drezy, Thanks. You're right, I'm still alive! It's gotten warmer around here now (Wchtr NY). I'll have to wait a few days to get back to the 20's.
And yet, the water temp is getting colder - down to about 49 F now.
Any recommendations for how long to hang out in the cold shower? (I usually do about 3 to 9 minutes).

# Thread: IS KLC a touch center?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-klc-a-touch-center.23251/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-06T18:55:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-klc-a-touch-center.23251/post-264611>

Do not wait until you have reached your goal to be proud of yourself, instead be proud of every step you take towards reaching that goal. Whenever you fail, never give up, learn the lessons and become better as a result of it.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The philosophy of touch is different for us all. You have to find your own. We can touch each other physically or mentally using overt and covert ways. Have you Do not focus on one option over another. Explore them all. I just did and it cut me and I bleed. Now I am letting you see me gutted.
Bleeding isn't optional for most of us, but maybe it should be? Not all wounds we create or bear are the bleeding kind but if they touch others they might be worthwhile.
Sometimes, reaching out and taking someone's hand is the beginning of a journey. At other times, it is allowing another to take yours.
Sometimes this journey begins when the touch is delivered wirelessly by an idea or a smile, or a word. No matter the mode, engage them with your power. Get so inside their head that they cannot help but feel your power.
Just as a life without love is incongruent, so is a life lived without the ability to touch. I am of the opinion it marks the genesis of a serious illness......it might be an illness from which you never entirely recover.
Tell your story.
Shout it. Write it.
Whisper it if you have to.
But tell it to the world.......
Some won't understand it.
Some will outright reject it.
But many will thank you for it.
And then the most
magical thing will happen in your life.
One by one, voices will start
whispering, 'Me, too.'
And your tribe will gather.
And you will never feel alone again.
With" a touch" of wisdom, can emerge the end of fears & disease.
The nose on your face is genetic. Intuition is epigenetic. That means it is wholly impacted by our environment. Our intuition is NOT always right but you can hone it to become the greatest tool in your epigenetic toolbox.
Its power and range are unmatched in nature.

Refuse to be ignored. Touch the masses in the ways that empower you. The soul that lives inside this body will not be ignored. I am here to stay and I am going to make you feel me in some way with or without your consent.
This is the story of the **March webinar of 2019**. It is quite a ride. It is a story of love that crashes into us via touch and when you are touched in this way you might gain some wisdom. Listen...........it might just change your life.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/25180098](https://www.patreon.com/posts/25180098?fbclid=IwAR1JLR28-HhkDEQhi_oO1BDaVqFztziReGo0gkwYaIBRepo-h1tCpxEQ_Wc)

### Cathy Hohmeyer — 2019-03-18T01:19:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-klc-a-touch-center.23251/post-265750>

As an OTR we learn about sensory perception but this takes the senses to the max.....Cohen says we have 54 natural senses that we have forgotten to "read" , to touch with . He calls it natural attraction... you may find it interesting [http://ecopsych.com/insight53senses.html](http://ecopsych.com/insight53senses.html)

# Thread: OPTIMAL eye fitness means no contacts or sunglasses.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-eye-fitness-means-no-contacts-or-sunglasses.23254/post-264680>

If you have to take your contacts out to get in the water when will your intuition begin to inform you that just having them touch your cornea any time is probably not a wise thing to be doing either? This is [#mitochondriacwisdom](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/mitochondriacwisdom?source=feed_text&epa=HASHTAG&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARCbibc7EsRlzR3GyfJ5_ZuUAADye-r4phtbdvVehXQ_TDkFs7v4WxcK4syczSTrFICot85yLeoP4wt7pAeZwxozViN2_zT733lc_LidwvWC3ZNu3O-BjnUzwTzqtyv5gcE1i8oDyfNiQw9-NLr5i3REhYf2idg_coz_wKkQdt5GGHVbEkkf0tA0PwBk_O_62QWXBd4So-jr7qyN0AbTA9ZPrEHoSGAW5qEHa9ljBH7RH23wgxQ5oAj07nrjHlBtnaHgjvr3oKntnt_TGy6T_sxaFicYDCmoYuseoQGaKHWJPOsl3L9KgxQv8QTGc4miN73QDd6sRXNVxZv5k7BClFaZcEUX-okNiJQtVkYb240qUHI2rvRtgAvXBAO-mOU7N8W-9AFAOqpy3-kZhA4cbHjmSOktABniNXzRqMCv5wHuixk&__tn__=%2ANK-R) Swimming is a fun way to enjoy your vacation. But keep your contact lenses out of the mix. Take lenses out before you swim to avoid getting germs in your eye that can lead to an infection. Jason the mitochondriac says,

This is what happens when you disconnect from nature and embrace way too much technology.
️ Talk to an ophthalmologist and they'll tell you they're seeing every eye condition at younger and younger ages.
The solution ⤵️
☀️ LOTS of sun during the day.
Darkness at night.
Plenty of seafood-based DHA.
LOTS of screen time breaks, as well as flicker reduction.
️ Blue screen filters and blue-blocking RaOptics glasses as artificial violet and blue light at ANY TIME of day is not good.

# Thread: IS COLD A Rx for kids with high risk exposure to blue light or nnEMF?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-a-rx-for-kids-with-high-risk-exposure-to-blue-light-or-nnemf.23259/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-08T18:12:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-a-rx-for-kids-with-high-risk-exposure-to-blue-light-or-nnemf.23259/post-264807>

As a man dying of cystic fibrosis how CT affects him.

He is one of my circle of 6 who I fight for. He is the most intuitive mammal I know.

This teacher is a modern day hero for kids everywhere. The greatest lesson of how to use Cold thermogenesis comes from another hero named Jeremy Thomley who uses CT to help limit the inflammation in his body from cystic fibrosis to stay alive.
JT said, " I studied Jackʼs Cold Thermogenesis Protocol and the breathing techniques until I could melt all the ice with my body heat. Soon I forgot about inflammation or being sick or any other reasons I thought I needed to get me in there. I started looking forward to it, I crave it."
He went on, " Sitting in the cold ice is your time to defragment your hard drive: it becomes a place where time slows and your ability to think slows. In fact, it is a time to not think - I think this is why Jack is so peaceful in the cold water. You will
struggle at first holding on to what you think is the real you: your schedule, your stress, your name, your life, your relationships. The cold will take it all from you. You are left with this empty pack that is so used to carrying troubles, doubt, cravings, hunger... nothing... its all gone. Cold erases the memory of many painful things because it causes you to focus on what matters most at that moment: SURVIVAL. It takes practice to surrender to this. Jack says he loves chaos and now I understand why he loves CT. CT is all about chaos, mental and biologic and otherwise. Some days when I come out I eventually question if I had a good session. The question feels objective like a scientist instead of personal like Iʼve wasted my time or somebody lied to me. Youʼll notice yourself fine-tuning the circumstances that occurred in your CT experience. Jack says most of the sparks of insight happen when he exits the cold and enters the sun/fire. In my case, the darkness was very important in my battle for my breath. CT forces me to realize how important my breath is when the air touches my lungs to bring me intuition." [https://www.msn.com/en-ca/weather/t...cold-not-escape-it/ar-BBUr6Gm?ocid=spartanntp](https://www.msn.com/en-ca/weather/topstories/we-get-soft-why-a-regina-teacher-says-kids-need-to-embrace-the-cold-not-escape-it/ar-BBUr6Gm?ocid=spartanntp)

My youngest loves CT. I let him acknowledge his own boundaries and go as far as he wants. Intuition tells him what to do. I don't need to.  Where mom leads they follow... when they're little. Once they've been brainwashed it's much harder.

### JVB — 2019-03-08T20:13:06+0200

### Kathy Spears — 2019-03-08T20:38:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-a-rx-for-kids-with-high-risk-exposure-to-blue-light-or-nnemf.23259/post-264825>

I often think about my time living in Sweden. Jack said I most likely became T1D because of my years there. I think about the daily habits the Swedes practiced that I did not. They were outside all day every day. Year round. I worked at a day care for a short time and the kids were brought to different parks every day where they played for a minimum of 2 hours, no matter how cold. I hated that part of my job!

The Swedes also enjoyed sitting in a sauna and then rolling in the snow. They also took beach vacations regularly throughout the winter. They soaked up the sun on their bare skin (nudity never an issue) every moment it peaked out. They swam in the freezing sea.

It’s all making sense now.

# Thread: Chris’s optimal journal.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.23261/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 33
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChrisnJr — 2019-03-08T22:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.23261/post-264839>

Hi everyone, new to the community thanks for having me.

\-My name is Chris J I’m a 28 year old male and I live in Cumberland Rhode Island. 15 miles from providence and 43 miles from Boston.

\-6,1

\-185 pounds (healthy weight is 205)

\-me and my identical twin brother were breastfed for 6 months. We were generally healthy children.

\-both diagnosed with ADHD as adolescents and took medication for 3 years.

\-became severely overweight as a child but lost over 100 pounds in high school through diet and exercise.

\-Grandmother died of lung cancer, also had diabetes, hyperthyroidism, long time cigarette smoker and standard American diet.

\-Mother has diabetes, hypertension, hyperthyroidism, high cholesterol. Anxiety depression. Half of thyroid removed, overweight, Standard American diet.

\-I’ve worked mainly as a construction worker/laborer for the last 5 years. Mostly outdoors.

\-Always been exposed to WiFi indoors and everyone in the house has an iPhone, two smart TVs, my brothers kids have iPads unfortunately.

\-I drink mostly distilled water with minerals added in via pink sea salt.

\-I have always been considered a health/nutrition fanatic by my family and friends.18 months ago I was in the best shape of my life physically. I was doing hard labor outside from sunrise to sunset and when the seasons allowed it I would train with heavy weights in my outdoor garage(no electricity/no WiFi) I would often take parts of my workouts outdoors when the weather allowed it. I felt like a million bucks and I looked like an NFL athlete. I was still dealing with some emotional issues such as mild anxiety and depression which have plagued me my whole life but I had no physical complaints whatsoever. I moved away from my home

In September or 2017 and everything changed, I got a job as a personal trainer and ironically my health and fitness began to fall apart. This was around the same

Time I moved into a new home. I’ve lost 20 pounds of muscle and my digestion is an absolute nightmare. My stomach used to be like a well-oiled machine, I always ate relatively healthy close to the paleo protocol but I could eat as much as I wanted and I always felt great. Now I am skinny and always bloated, I’m

Losing weight no matter what I eat. There is nothin that won’t give me digestive issues. Even just drinking water gives me belching fits that last hours.

\-I strongly believe that the change in environment (new job new home) are what’s to blame for my collapsed health. I think overdoing it in the gym may have also been a strong contributor. I feel strongly that I’m suffering from leaky gut and possibly adrenal fatigue. My stomach acid is absolutely gone. I’m

Also starting to notice some thyroid/ Autoimmune issues setting in.

The following are list of symptoms

\-belching/bloating/gas

\-shortness of breath/Hiatial hernia

\-aches and pains/pins and needles

\-always cold

\-bizarre sweating patterns

\-mood swings/apathy/anxiety/severe depression

\-constipation/diarrhea

\-joint pain

\-insatiably hungry for carbs and salt

I eat healthier than anyone I know but I feel Worse than anyone I know.I’m definitely going to start incorporating more seafood and bone broth into my life. I also just took a job as a construction worker that will have me out in the sun all day everyday. I have done a 23 and me test and I am waiting on the results of GI-Map, Organic Acids Test \[OAT\] and MycoTox and GPL-TOX) tests. I don’t know if these will tell me anything and I really don’t know what I should be looking for. I apologize for my ignorance but I am desperate to learn more and any help would be most appreciated. I have been close to giving up recently. My friends, family and wife think I’m a crazy hypochondriac. I will do whatever is necessary to regain my health. Thank you all in advance. I am so happy to have found jack and this community and am looking forward to journey of becoming my old self.

### Katie Durham — 2019-03-09T18:28:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.23261/post-264920>

Chris, welcome. What struck me was the new home. I think you might want to invest in one or two meters to identify where the nnEMF hotspots are. Or hire an expert to show you where you'll need to make adjustments. If you're trainer position is in a gym you'll have far less options to do anything about that but you could at least check how serious that environment is. There are tri-mode meters (measure RF, AC magnetic, and AC electric) like the TriField TF2, Acousticom 2, and ENV RD-10. For dirty electricity you would need something like the Stetzerizer microsurge meter. There are good online tutorials on how to use them.

### ChrisnJr — 2019-03-10T13:34:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.23261/post-265003>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=264987)
>
> Depends on the environmental context and your epigenetic toolbox.

I can say that for the first 27 years of my life, (up until December 2017) my physical health was not an issue at all in this area. My digestion was on point, I felt ten feet tall. I always struggled with anxiety and depression particularly in the winter and was diagnosed ADHD as a child which makes a lot of sense after watching and reading all of your content and reading the books you’ve recommended. Aside from the digestive and autoimmune issues popping up is my general inability to heal and recover. I used to be able to heal from any injury in a few days. Now if I get hurt at work or softball it seems to never go away. My health did a complete flip when I moved in 2017, even though I only moved about 10 miles away. This was also around the same time I stopped working outside which I am now doing again. I don’t know if my new home and job were to blame for this or if something intrinsically changed in this area. I am willing to take any steps necessary to gain optimal health here and if I cannot do that I will have to move.

It would break my heart to have to leave my wife and brother and all the kids but the life I have now is not worth living. Everyone around me agrees that I am a shell of the person I used to be.

It’s literally non-stop. I also have intermittentshortness of breath and very slow GI motility. I’m eating seafood for breakfast and dinner, watching the sunrise everyday(I work outside under the sun all day). Wearing my blue blockers after dark. ### Michael Thompson — 2019-04-21T18:49:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.23261/post-268562>

Hi. You might want to dump the avocadoes, since they only grow in tropical climate, so a mismatch for us in high latitudes. I have begun to have digestive issues, a dull pain on my left side of my gut, constipation, gas. my diet is almost perfect, so i am suspecting it is nnEMF. I found there is over 20 cell towers, plus a military training base, all with in a 3 mile radius of the house I am living in, so I am moving! easier for me since I am single and not a home owner. Good luck, I hope you find what you need!

### ChrisnJr — 2019-07-01T15:42:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.23261/post-273533>

18 months since I first started to notice a decline in my health. 4 months since I discovered Dr Kruse’s teachings and started researching mitochondrial health. Since April 1st I have worked outside under the sun every day including most weekends. I’m lucky enough to have a job that is constantly outside usually in rural, Suburban areas and I am able to work without a shirt on which I do everyday. I make a point to watch the sunrise every morning. I spend 90 percent of my waking time Outdoors including exercising barefoot in my back yard 3-5 times a week followed by an ice cold shower.

Im doing the best I can with water, drinking mostly Voss and Aqua Panna from glass bottles. Eating 6-10 oysters and a half jar of Salmon roe every day. Along with raw milk, yogurt and butter, pastured eggs, Bone broth with coconut oil and mackerel for additional DHA. I have noticed an incredible improvement in my strength and body composition, I’ve lost almost 20 pounds and very lean while feeling as strong as ever.

I am however still plagued by stomach issues and mood swings/depression/lethargy. Sometimes I have to struggle just to get through my workday. The fatigue is like nothing I’ve ever felt. Not sure if I should be adding some seasonal carbs into the mix.

My digestion is still a mess most of the time.
Zero regularity, constantly belching and hiatus hernia symptoms. I am eating a huge breakfast then another large meal around 5 pm and that is it. Before my health problems I was a regular coffee consumer, 1-3 cups daily but I have almost cut it out completely. Ironically the coffee used to help boost my mood and energy while also aiding digestion but I realize how ignorant I was to be using it as a crutch.

I was working with a functional medicine practitioner before I discovered this forum who tells me I have mold toxicity causing my chronic fatigue and constant gas/stomach problems but I know Dr kruse’s take on all that. I feel like I am doing everything I can from a quantum perspective. Also blue blocking every night and sleeping in total darkness with the WiFi off. I am astounded by how much stronger and leaner I have become but I would almost trade those benefits for quality digestion and great energy. My family and peers look at me and assume that I must feel great is back but sadly that is not yet the case.

I am extremely thankful to be a part of this group and having discovered this information I have definitely seen the value. I have always felt a strong connection to nature and now more than ever I have hope which is something I could not always say over the past 18 months. If anyone can offer any insight as always thank you in advance

For months I’ve been feeling a large amount of tension in my stomach and i can feel gas or something moving through my intestines very clearly. It can also be heard so loudly that people next to me wonder if I’m ok. This is usually accompanied by very a unusually pain/sensation in my extremities, Especially my left foot, for whatever reason.

I’m also bothered by bouts of uncontrollable belching. It doesn’t seem to matter what I eat, either one of these problems can be brought on from just a drink of water. Ironically I almost never actually have bad gas anymore. I used to be extremely gassy but now I hardly ever pass gas, though when I do it provides instant palpable relief to my prostate and urinary discomfort. It can make life extremely uncomfortable and it doesn’t seem to want to go away. Has anyone ever experienced anything like this.

### Zehlersan — 2026-02-23T10:00:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chris%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.23261/post-367439>

Try not to drink too much plain water before during or after a meal.it will dilute you're stomach acid too much and cause digestion issues.that was my experience. If you do need something while eating try a sparkling mineral water it's usually more acidic helping aid in digestion. Warm water and half a lemon 1hr before a meal can boost acidity as well, or 1-2oz of water with a 1/8th teaspoon of cayenne pepper 1hr before meal does the same. I've learned the last 2 remedies mentioned from Barbara O'Neil. I hope this helps. Blessings to you brother on your journey.

couple of things I’m wondering about

\-my latitude and location in general and if it is possible to get healthy here. I’m about 15 minutes away from providence Rhode Island. I’m willing to do whatever it takes to achieve optimum health but moving would be a last resort as I have deep roots here.

\-leaky gut protocol. Is raw goat kefir a
Good substance to drink or should dairy be avoided all together? Really need some help
Getting my gut back in order. (I plan on making my sun exposure and mitigating blue light and non native emf my first priority, just looking for some
Help in the food department.

\-water. What’s the deal with water in my area? I have an at-home distiller, would that be a good water source?

Thank you all.

I’m also wondering why I don’t have access
To “ask jack” yet. Can anyone shed some
Light on this. Sorry for being a newbie.

### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-09T05:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.23262/post-264867>

Hi Chris welcome to the forum. I'm actually quite new here myself but have been following Jack, just as an observer, figured it is time to speak out, help out, whatever I can do.
It's always tricky giving advice because, as Jack would say, it is an N=1 world, so we all have to somewhat find what will work for ourselves. That being said there are also often similarities since we are all exposing ourselves to the same unhealthy habits and environment.
Yes on the distilled water, especially if your other options come from the city water supply.

### Ronald Waters — 2019-03-09T05:58:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.23262/post-264868>

With the gut issues, when I need to get myself back on track, I find it easiest to fast for a few days and then I can start back on food with a very strict and disciplined program. You can use C8 coconut oil for energy as it goes straight to your liver. Eat simple and put as much time between meals as possible. Whenever your get isn't working it has an opportunity to heal. No sugar, No sweeteners, low carb.
I live in Iowa and I think in the north we have to be a lot more intentional about getting sunlight. I spent 1 month this winter down south in the sun and my Vitamin D is only a 39, with normal range being 30-100. Have you read the Leptin Rx? Are you doing any cold therapy?

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-09T16:14:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.23262/post-264900>

Leaky gut = melanopsin issue......therefore it is the environment. Hack it to understand why it is happening then adapt.

Since this all started I’ve lost all my strength and muscle while gaining a bunch of abdominal fat. I’ve lost almost 25 pounds and can’t gain weight to save my life. I’m just wondering if any dietary protocol I should be following while hacking my environment. I’ve definitely developed food sensitivities. Should I follow the Leptin RX? As always thank you for your wisdom.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-09T17:36:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-the-light-diet-begin.23267/post-264910>

It is not how Darwin or Watson or Crick envisioned.

few million years of chilly water and sunlight in the photic zone of the sea and you get new genes not passed on from mom or Dad. That is the basis of my OSF 3 blog. Another prediction checked off. Here’s the quantum evolution of a new gene from non-coding DNA, step by step. It's a gene for antifreeze in Arctic fish. This should be very interesting for neo-Darwinists because it destroys the narrative of Watson and Crick. It is the 1st time molecular biology has to admit that Lamarck's ideas are not quackery. It shows the definitive truth that de novo gene creation EXISTS in life. It has to make the curious out there wonder what other quantum functions non-coding genes have that we are unaware of? #BlackSwanwisdom The colder the water the more DHA is in the seafood............think that matters too? YEP. [https://www.pnas.org/content/116/10/4400](https://www.pnas.org/content/116/10/4400) …

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-09T17:38:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-did-the-light-diet-begin.23267/post-264911>

# Thread: Thought Experiment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-experiment.23271/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MITpowered26 — 2019-03-10T03:13:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-experiment.23271/post-264971>

The above video is of a light bulb powered by what I believe is **electromagnetism**. I will test my knowledge (after googling for the last bit) by trying to explain how the light bulb turns on...and then relate it to quantum biology.

Water - In this case tap water I assume, fundamentally made of H20 **molecules** (**atomic** structures of 2 hydrogens attached to one oxygen).

Add salt: Dare I say, a "phenomenon" occurs whereby the salt (sodium chloride) mixed with H20 forms an **electrolyte** substrate conducive to **electrical current** (electrical current or electricity being either **electrons** or **ion** flow) in this case, ion flow.

The wire that is stripped at one end forms the **electrodes** (two different materials that relatively attract positive (**protons**) or negative (electrons)). As the electrodes touch water, the positive electrode attracts the negatively charged Chloride (Cl-) which recieved a transfer of electrons while in solution) and the negative electrode attracts the Na+ (positive charged sodium which has given up an electron in solution). The cross travel of the ions (Na+, Cl-) creates electricity ,which in itself, I believe would power the light bulb, by completing the circuit (basically chloride is carrying the extra electron from one electrode to the other), hence electrical current?

We have our battery!

This is the part that is a bit confusing for me. The 3 conditions whereby **magnets (**IE special orderly material that has its own energy fields - magnetic fields) are when the magnetic and electric fields are variably , OR magnetic field is variable and electrical field constant, OR electrical field variable and magnetic field constant.

In this case, electrical field is variable? as the electrolyte is moving ions/electrons. I believe the magnet is boosting the current here .

EDIT: ADD BELOW.. Sorry I accidentally posted premature.

So then I guess electro magnetic fields are basically a phenomenon whereby electrical and magnetic fields COMBINE to create super energy? lol IE the electro magnetic spectrum.

APPLICATION:

The ocean versus fresh water. Salt water is more conductive. I'm guessing from a quantum biology perspective, with metals in us, hemoglobin made from iron, etc, there is magnetism and thus we are electrically charging ourselves like the light bulb in water (crude crude connection). Add in light (which is part of the electromagnetic spectrum) i'm starting to see that energy transfer (movement of energy, electrons), is the spice, the key, IS life. SUN emits electromagnetic energy ,stored ideally in ocean water, we receive this energy via water AND OR sunlight.

Its starting to make sense.. if only very crudely.

\*\*FEEL FREE TO CORRECT ANY OF THIS LANGUAGE, I KNOW I DONT HAVE IT CORRECT FOR SURE\*\*\*

J

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-10T04:13:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-experiment.23271/post-264977>

Light is an electromagnetic wave that always has its electric field and magnetic field orthogonal to one another.......uneless man fucks it up and engineers it like they have with 5G.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-at-night.23274/post-265057>

### Mike David — 2019-03-11T16:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading-at-night.23274/post-265118>

> [Kathy Spears said:](/goto/post?id=265110)
>
> When I read about the power grid being turned off at night, I'm trying to figure out the best way to accomplish this. Do I just go to the breaker box and turn off everything for each bedroom? I need an alarm clock (not using cell!!) so I'm not sure how to do this. I've seen the equipment you can buy that is like a zone kill switch...
> So much to figure out! It would be easier if I had even a little understanding of electricity!

Have someone go into the room you want killed. You go to the breaker box and start flipping the single 15 or 20 amp ones (numbers are on the breaker handle) until the room goes off. Put a sticker on the ones you want off then repeat the process yourself every night. Don’t forget when you wake up that it’s your fault the powers out not electric company. Make sure you turn off sensitive electronic equipment before throwing breakers on and off. If your lucky each breaker should be labeled on the cover door. Make sure you didn’t cut off the fridge, sump pump, heater ect... along with your bedroom.
