### Skip Platt — 2019-03-18T20:05:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skips-journey-to-optimal.23333/post-265815>

Hello! I'm Skip from Mobile, AL and have been a Gold member since last year although I've followed Jack for the past 4 or 5 years. I figured my first post here should be my optimal journal, so here goes...

I was born 3 months premature and stayed in an incubator for 2 months before going home. I had asthma as a child and stayed sick often. I’ve lived in Mobile, AL my entire life and grew up waterskiing on the river almost every weekend.

I grinded my teeth a lot while sleeping as a child.

As a teenager I had pretty bad acne and took various antibiotics quite frequently as well as Accutane. I was diagnosed with degenerative disk disease at the age of 15. After several cortisone shots for back pain as a teenager, I felt one bubble in my throat somehow and instantly tasted a metallic taste. It was at this point I began to dig deeper to get to the root of issues and stop using “bandaids.” I began to see a chiropractor and have had significant improvement with no back pain ever since.

I had recurring sinus infections 5 years ago and developed pneumonia 4 years ago. I was prescribed and took Levaquin and three months later tore my ACL. It was at this point I began to dig deeper into the world of optimal recovery, energy and resilience and began drinking the “Bulletproof Kool-Aid.” Along the pathway to becoming a Bulletproof Coach I discovered Dr. Jack and realized that all the biohacking and supplements were just “band aids” from the opposite end of the spectrum.

During this time period I had my Vitamin D, sex hormones and much more tested for the first time at the age of 36 and began doing bioidentical testosterone. In hindsight, I would’ve liked to have optimized my Vitamin D first and then rechecked the hormones before starting. I’ve most recently stopped the testosterone therapy to see if I can optimize it on my own with Dr. Jack’s help/educational consult, etc.

I have an internal hemorrhoid that bleeds substantially, and I’ve recently noticed a connection between drinking too much coffee/MCT oil. This has led to developing iron-deficiency anemia. I’ve cut way back on the coffee “cocktail” and skip it occasionally, and have noticed some slow improvement as I continue to check lab values.

My 23andme maternal haplogroup is H10a1.

I walk outside barefoot every morning and stare in the direction of the sun. I get lots of naked sun several times per week. I’ve slowly reversed my myopia from -4.25 to -3.75. I wear contacts for at least half the day and have recently switched to Cooper Biofinity brand as it supposedly does not block UV. I eat a dozen raw oysters 2-3 times per week.

I have a LOT of lab tests, but I'm not sure they would be helpful right now as they're 4 months old or older.

### Cheryl. — 2019-03-19T09:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skips-journey-to-optimal.23333/post-265863>

Your labs will give a starting point. Nice read and I am impressed with the healing! Good luck

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-19T14:30:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skips-journey-to-optimal.23333/post-265885>

Welcome, Skip. I love your perspective flip. We all go through them.

Life is unbelievably ironic at times. In my own existence, I have complained because certain situations I have faced were like awesome but filled with potholes, kinda like a sweet smelling rose bush but the thorns limited me from fully going all in. With time and reflection, I began to rejoice because I realized the thorns slowed me down to realize there was a better path of understanding. Thorns have roses for a reason. [#perspectiveflip](https://twitter.com/hashtag/perspectiveflip?src=hash)

### Skip Platt — 2020-02-10T20:32:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skips-journey-to-optimal.23333/post-285836>

I asked my mom about her and her mom's health history.

My mom was exposed to radiation for years as she was in X-ray/nuclear medicine. During this time she had a chemical burn to her throat and sinuses from X-ray processor chemicals. More recently she's had an appendectomy, and the appendix leaked out a mucin type fluid while undergoing removal. She continues to have liver and renal cysts. She also had pituitary and adrenal adenomas. She's had 2 ruptured disks. She had Epstein-Barr/Mono when she was 16 years old and 2 miscarriages before I was born.

My grandmother had agenisis of the corpus callosum. She had her gallbladder removed. Ruptured disk surgery. Had other surgeries to remove ovaries, tonsils and sinus surgery. She had congestive heart failure and frequent blackout spells along with numerous stokes and TIA's in her last years.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2019-03-19T11:25:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photosynthesis-also-depends-on-ira.23338/post-265874>

Searched for this topic, but didn't see it posted before:

[https://phys.org/news/2018-06-photosynthesis.html](https://phys.org/news/2018-06-photosynthesis.html)

**New type of photosynthesis discovered**

Looks like photosynthesis can also depend on IRA in some instances, contrary to the previous belief that visible light was exclusively responsible.

Even indoor growing LEDs contain just visible light most of the time. Seems like humans and plants have more in common than previously thought...

### Lahelada — 2019-03-19T12:39:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/formula-for-a-preterm-baby.23339/post-265875>

Does anybody know of a preterm formula that is less processed, has good results or any pointers ? A friend of mine In Spain had a baby at 34 weeks born at 1400g who is likely to be released from hospital next week. The mothers milk is not sufficient. (They are quantum orientated so will mind her light etc . ) Many thanks for any suggestions they can run by the pediatrician.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-19T14:25:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/formula-for-a-preterm-baby.23339/post-265883>

I would add a ton of the brain specific nutrients I talked about in my book.

### LieselK — 2019-03-19T14:55:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/formula-for-a-preterm-baby.23339/post-265888>

Can they get donor milk? There is a lot of milk-sharing here in the US. I also know people who have made their own "formula" with goat's milk as the base as well since it's closest in composition to human milk. I guess in many ways this was a lot easier when we stuck with our tribes/cultures and could nurse each other's babies as needed where environmental and genetic factors were the same. Praying for your friend [@Lahelada](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6531/). I'm sure that momma could use extra love. It's the worst stress when we can't feed our babies easily or enough.

### Phosphene — 2019-03-19T15:14:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/formula-for-a-preterm-baby.23339/post-265889>

I agree with all above. In addition, is she familiar with La Leche League?

The group support is invaluable and can provide resources for things like lactation consultants and local folk remedies.

### Bastian — 2019-03-20T03:03:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-d-dminder-snps.23345/post-265952>

Hi guys,
so my Vitamin D from September 18 was 29ng/ml. Fast forward to March 2019 and it is 53ng/ml. I spend huge amount of time outside in New Zealand summer and never got burned since I built up my solar callus which is awesome.
Yet I am disappointed since I was using DMinder diligently for the entire period and according to the app I should be around 85ng/ml. So either the app is pretty inaccurate or my SNPs are playing into it or both.

According to my SNPs, I have a VDR Taq AA +/+, which should inhibit my VitD receptor function. Assuming DMinder is correct the SNP would make around 40% less efficient making VitD. That is somewhat scary...
My SNPs are below and my maternal haplogroup is H1a3. # Thread: PANS/ PANDAS/ Autoimmune encephalitis

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sass — 2019-03-20T13:06:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pans-pandas-autoimmune-encephalitis.23348/post-265983>

Hi Jack,
I've been looking into the world of PANS and autoimmune encephalitis. There is now no doubt in my mind that this is what my son (diagnosed with autism/ epilepsy/ bowel problems) has and I suspect multiple triggers (candida and clostridia for starters). Interestingly one of the diagnostic tests for PANS is to show that full dose ibuprofen reduces behavioural symptoms (autism/ ocd/ tourettes type symptoms) as this is partial evidence that these are caused by inflammation - when I did this trial with my son his seizures disappeared so I think they might also have inflammation as their cause). I just wondered if you had any words of wisdom. Obviously there's not a lot of overlap with that world and this one in terms of knowledge and wisdom at present. I also feel sure that this is what is actually going on for a lot of people diagnosed with autism.
Thanks, Sara

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-20T13:15:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pans-pandas-autoimmune-encephalitis.23348/post-265985>

All of those are linked to the surrounding environment you and he was in before the disease manifested. You blame the bugs.......I can't because I know how bug and our immune system work with nnEMF.

# Thread: Possible CT struggle due to 5g?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-ct-struggle-due-to-5g.23356/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Luisa88 — 2019-03-21T08:49:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-ct-struggle-due-to-5g.23356/post-266086>

I just started doing ice baths once a week (I did go from face dunks and cold showers so skipped the ice on chest) and I feel like they are messing with my system. I’ve only done 3 total for 10 minutes each.

I am lean but was doing them for fatigue and anxiety. It seems I’m worse and my liver is sluggish. Is this likely detox or possibly because I’m in Perth Western Australia and 5G is rolling out as we speak.

I tried taking a b vitamin (desperation) and that induced severe anxiety (I am prone to supplement intolerance and poor effects).

Has anyone else experienced this? Not sure if it’s something to just push through or back off whilst living here

### LieselK — 2019-03-21T14:32:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-ct-struggle-due-to-5g.23356/post-266096>

> [Luisa88 said:](/goto/post?id=266086)
>
> I just started doing ice baths once a week (I did go from face dunks and cold showers so skipped the ice on chest) and I feel like they are messing with my system. I’ve only done 3 total for 10 minutes each.
>
> I am lean but was doing them for fatigue and anxiety. It seems I’m worse and my liver is sluggish. Is this likely detox or possibly because I’m in Perth Western Australia and 5G is rolling out as we speak.
>
> I tried taking a b vitamin (desperation) and that induced severe anxiety (I am prone to supplement intolerance and poor effects).
>
> Has anyone else experienced this? Not sure if it’s something to just push through or back off whilst living here

How is your redox? Remember redox before detox. So if CT is causing detox you aren't ready for then no good. Are you eating high fat and high protein prior to CT? I think this is sooooo important when you're lean. Indoor CT I always do in the evening which induces the best sleep. Outdoors in the sun I do after lunch or bfast depending on sun and temp. Maybe shorten the CT session to only a few min as well.

### Luisa88 — 2019-03-21T14:49:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-ct-struggle-due-to-5g.23356/post-266097>

Thanks Liesel,

Probably not eating enough fat before actually. I was doing them at midday and then lying in the sun. After I would crash for an hour - completely fall asleep like a die off reaction. So I think you’re right.

I think my redox must still suck. I’ve been doing AM sun for the last 2 months, changed my drinking water (donut hole), eating more seafood than ever, take progesterone, being very careful with light where I can but my job is pretty stressful and under fluorescent lights and lots of medical equipment so figure I’ll take a while to improve my redox.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-21T15:04:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/possible-ct-struggle-due-to-5g.23356/post-266099>

It is not a possible...........it is what it is. CHANGE it by altering how you think about it.

Keep doing new stuff your old self would never do.......this is how N =1 change happens

Optimal health is a choice.......choose wrong and you can lose. What you choose also chooses you. You are creating the new you through your new choices, even now. If you want the new you to manifest well choose wisely my fellow Black Swans in training. [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qA1nGPM9yHA...](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qA1nGPM9yHA&fbclid=IwAR2_3Fp_bZhVPYvztIHbutqMJonCG_I_3PzwhCvsgH6wn-Np8ZBWF9Tf6qY)

You can’t get better in the same environment that made you sick. Some people are toxic and draining.

Cut them too.

Today’s Black Swan PSA: Letting go doesn't mean that you don't care about someone anymore. It's just realizing that the only person you really have control over is yourself.

### caroline — 2019-03-24T02:29:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/men-at-kruse-longevity-center-junkmaintenance-program-is-unleashed.23357/post-266310>

We met a young man \[88 years young\] at a local pub. He had just finished 11 hours on his tractor - ploughing the fields or something similar.

He is articulate, strong, healthy and smart \[and my personal favourite\] cheeky!.......been on the farm his whole life. He left school at 13 yo to help his Dad on the farm.

As we were walking back to our camper in the dark - we couldn't stop talking about him ....BTW - his last name is Jolly!

He is thinking of buying a camper like ours and seeing the bits of Australia that he hasn't yet ....and that isn't much let me tell you. He has given Tony and I renewed energy and a huge smile on our faces.

Arthur Jolly was such an inspiration and revelation to both of us.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-coq10-cause-negative-side-effects.23369/post-266247>

I hear great things about COQ10 but every time I have tried a 200 mg pill I feel winded and tired the next day. Any ideas why?

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-23T14:40:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-coq10-cause-negative-side-effects.23369/post-266263>

### Michael CULLEN — 2019-03-23T15:00:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-coq10-cause-negative-side-effects.23369/post-266267>

It is not stable under low oxygen conditions. Idebenone is better.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-23T15:36:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-coq10-cause-negative-side-effects.23369/post-266270>

Sluggish autophagy is a sign of altered Calcium ion flows in your cell due to a lack of control of voltage gates by solar EMF. The most common cause of the lack of control is nnEMF which allows too much calcium efflux. This alters oxygen levels, the NAD+ couple, and free radical signaling in mitochondria to lead to higher heteroplasmy and disease tied to leptin resistance. Usually, melanopsin damage occurs as a TOPOLOGIC collateral effect of the light stress as the nnEMF.

HOW DOES THE PHYSICS LOOK FOR SLUGGISH AUTOPHAGY?

Ions in any static and uniform magnetic field will typically move in a circle with a cyclotron frequency determined by its charge q, its mass m, and the strength of the magnetic field called B. The operation of a cyclotron is based on the fact that the period of the motion of a charged particle in a uniform magnetic field is independent of the velocity of the particle, as can be seen in the following mathematical derivation:

F = ma

qvB = mv2/r

Solving for r:

r = mv/qB

Now find the period, T:

T = 1/f = 2π/ω = 2πr/v = 2πmv/qBv

Since the velocities' cancel:

T = 2πm/qB

The “Cyclotron Frequency” f (c) immediately follows and is:

F© = qB/2πm

These ion cyclotron resonance effects were extensively investigated in Abe Blackman’s lab but elsewhere in the 1980s up until today. All laboratories who have studied these effects found that calcium cyclotron frequency directly affects calmodulin regulation of the calcium ion concentration in solution and a host of biologic functions. These are all tied to marked alterations of voltage-dependent calcium channels when the field strength is above 30 Hz. To date, 23 studies have been done and repeated this finding since 1980. Anything that affects calmodulin by definition affects magnesium ion concentrations as well in a cell. This is why an altered electric and magnetic field by nnEMF's are primordial to calcium and magnesium regulation. Taking more calcium or magnesium do nothing for the intracellular voltage channels. Using calcium channel blockers, however, can help. What is the best calcium channel on Earth? **SUNLIGHT**

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-24T17:45:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-266347>

They say women's beauty fades quickly like a fragile flower and men age gracefully like a good wine. Indeed, especially today a lot of ladies hit the wall before 40.

But I have seen some women who seem to age well and defy aging (some nuns come to mind). I think there is more to it than passage of years and biology and physics.

A woman sweet of temperament and pure of heart can be ageless.

### JanSz — 2019-03-24T17:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-266348>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=266313)
>
> still striving, still learning, still very curious and loving life.......

Caroline;
You are my guru.
gurvi
\-------
What do you call a female guru?
According to a handy Sanskrit dictionary from the library, the **female** equivalent of a **guru** (गुरु) is a gurvi (गुर्वी).
This is confirmed to an extent in a couple of places.
It is also interesting to note that **guru** literally means grave or weighty and is cognate with the English word grave.
.

### caroline — 2019-03-25T02:34:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-266370>

### LieselK — 2019-03-25T23:49:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-266442>

>
>

Maybe "late" is actually just the right time. My AP English teacher was retiring after my senior year and she told us that many people will say that the high school or college years are the best years of your life, but she disagreed. She said life gets better and better and she was very much looking forward to her next chapter. No regrets!

### caroline — 2019-03-26T09:01:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-266460>

I always remember years and years ago .....some one wanted to go back to school and get a degree. She was 50 and thought it was too late ....because she would be 55 when she completed her degree.

Someone very wise said ......you are going be 55 regardless!

I learned that lesson probably 35 years ago and have never forgotten it.

It sounds so simple doesn't it ......but when we know better ....

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=266460)
>
> Someone very wise said ......you are going be 55 regardless!

Love this! I'm going to be 33 this year.

### Jenelle — 2019-04-08T20:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267629>

> [Christine\_L said:](/goto/post?id=267626)
>
> Love this! I'm going to be 33 this year.

I've seen this all over the nursing forum in the past. Seems like people showed up almost daily saying, "Am I too old?"

And everybody's like, dude ... you're going to age no matter what. If it's your dream ~ go for it.

It's not a thought I even had before starting back to school. And I mean, not once. It seems really bizarre to me that so many women ask about this. So my thought has always been ... I guess if you have to ask the question ... then maybe you ARE too old?! Because that number's just a number. I think it all comes down to your state of mind.

### Christine_L — 2019-04-09T16:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267677>

Its because we put limits on ourselves based on social norms like age

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-04-09T18:13:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267698>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=266442)
>
> Maybe "late" is actually just the right time. My AP English teacher was retiring after my senior year and she told us that many people will say that the high school or college years are the best years of your life, but she disagreed. She said life gets better and better and she was very much looking forward to her next chapter. No regrets!

Well, I am old enough to reflect on life and consider what it has taught me.

I too believe late teens/early 20s are indeed the best years of our lives, at least for many of us.

What we lack in wisdom and experience at a youthful age we make up for in body vitality and zest for living (although in today's toxic polluted 5G world even some young people seem to be short-changed in those youth gifts-- even suicides are increasing). But traditionally, everything is fresh, new, and exciting to young people, and they may have lofty ideals they want to pursue (before everyday life slams us down into dutiful compliance).

Our youthful spirits are ready to fly because we are not weighed down by baggage of emotional scars of life's bumps and bruises.

That said, we sadly in most cases lack vision and judgement to put those special years to good use (and many kids are trapped in the educational gulag into as late as mid-20s-- that's sure to breed conformity). By the time we have accumulated judgement and wisdom the body is visibly going into senescence (although we can certainly slow down aging through perfect living habits). _**Ain't life ironic.**_

### caroline — 2019-04-09T23:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267734>

beautifully said ^^^^^

However, I do feel we can make every moment "the best" If we are wide eyed ......and open to all possibilities.

### Lahelada — 2019-04-09T23:44:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267736>

You are as young as your faith, as old as your doubt; as young as your self-confidence, as old as your fear; as young as your hope, as old as your despair. ~Douglas MacArthur

### caroline — 2019-04-09T23:47:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267737>

I just went back and read Jack's original post....... how bloody fantastic those words are for me!

To realise our heart's desires later in life and to keep learning, and striving and being curious.

Colour me purple!

### LieselK — 2019-04-09T23:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267738>

> [ElectricUniverse said:](/goto/post?id=267698)
>
> But traditionally, everything is fresh, new, and exciting to young people, and they may have lofty ideals they want to pursue (before everyday life slams us down into dutiful compliance).
>
> Our youthful spirits are ready to fly because we are not weighed down by baggage of emotional scars of life's bumps and bruises.

I guess it's always been different for me because I had plenty of bumps and bruises by the time I got to college. In recently finally healing from those wounds I am ready to fly at age 36. Life does feel fresh, exciting and new. I'm glad for that. I've always been told I was wise for my age and always been the mother-figure in my circles. It's about time I caught up in age what my mind was already doing for so long. It feels \*right\*. Now to match up in age, mind AND body! Florida sun here I come!

### LieselK — 2019-04-09T23:52:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267739>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=267737)
>
> I just went back and read Jack's original post....... how bloody fantastic those words are for me!
>
> To realise our heart's desires later in life and to keep learning, and striving and being curious.
>
> Colour me purple!

My Grandmother-in-law recently died at the age of 98. She was recently baptized (never too late!) and was such a joy despite her grumpy old ways  I wish we had been in a place to open our home to her at any point so she could watch her great-grandbabies grow up. I would love to live inter-generationally and watch the strengths, struggles and joys play out.

It's never too late to learn and strive and seek. I have no clue what I'll do in my post-children life, but the possibilities seem endless! Wherever life takes me, I'll be there!

I was a total fucking knucklehead when I was young. Now just semi knuckleheaded. Much happier now that i dont know as much.

### caroline — 2019-04-10T04:28:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267752>

One day ...here on the forum...I tried to suggest to Matt Maruca that he \[and people of his generation\] were missing a lot if they didn't engage with us oldies, as well as people their own age.

Matty thought I was offended but, actually, I think it is quite sad when young people can't entangle with an older generation .....they are missing a ton of wisdom and incredible adventures that they can't even imagine! They are missing so much ......

Does the younger generation not want to entangle with the older generation or is it that they don't know how?

### recoen — 2019-04-10T05:24:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267755>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=267752)
>
> One day ...here on the forum...I tried to suggest to Matt Maruca that he \[and people of his generation\] were missing a lot if they didn't engage with us oldies, as well as people their own age.
>
> Matty thought I was offended but, actually, I think it is quite sad when young people can't entangle with an older generation .....they are missing a ton of wisdom and incredible adventures that they can't even imagine! They are missing so much ......
>
> Does the younger generation not want to entangle with the older generation or is it that they don't know how?

I have and continue to prefer and enjoy spending time with those older (much when I was a child) than me. I enjoy listening and learning from them. Also, many have hilarious stories. I have never felt “on the same wavelength” as my contemporaries. My favorite is listening and watching as old buddies talk about their stories . I use old as chronologically older than me.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-04-10T13:54:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267765>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=266266)
>
> Don't waste your time ladies of the world. _**You might wake up one day and realize when you finally got the wings to fly you'll have nowhere else to go to......**_[#BlackSwanWisdom](https://twitter.com/hashtag/BlackSwanWisdom?src=hash)
>
>
>
> { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Have you been giving the best of you to a group or person who really does not appreciate you? How has that affected your desires? Have you ever thought to ask yourself the difficult questions in your own life? If not, begin to do so if you want to become a supreme leader of yourself. 2019 has become the year I have decided to actually change this right now in my own life.......
A real leader of men should always hold themselves responsible for a higher standard than any one of the tribe expect of them. They should never at any time excuse or pity themselves. They should be hard masters to themselves, and be tolerating and lenient to everybody else.
There's no need to be perfect in order to inspire others, let other people be inspired by how you deal with your failures and imperfections.

Maybe the journey isn't so much about becoming anything.
Maybe it's about unbecoming everything that isn't really so
you can be who you were meant to be in the first place? Consider the alternatives when your desires are weak. People go to far greater lengths to avoid what they fear than to obtain what they desire. Put aside your fears and worries about what others will think of you and just serve others with your message and your mission. Who are you good for if you are not good enough for you? Ask this question of yourself every day when your desires are weak. “You” is the most important word in any conversation. Minimize the “I” and the “Me” and focus on the “you.” You are the CEO of you. Never forget that. I want you to jump off your scary precipice with me right now and find our wings on the way down. Let us begin that process today, together.

### KrusinWitchie — 2019-04-10T16:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267770>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=267752)
>
> One day ...here on the forum...I tried to suggest to Matt Maruca that he \[and people of his generation\] were missing a lot if they didn't engage with us oldies, as well as people their own age.
>
> Matty thought I was offended but, actually, I think it is quite sad when young people can't entangle with an older generation .....they are missing a ton of wisdom and incredible adventures that they can't even imagine! They are missing so much ......
>
> Does the younger generation not want to entangle with the older generation or is it that they don't know how?

I think it’s both. They aren’t quite capable, so to make it easier they convince themselves it’s because old people are \_\_\_\_\_\_ (fill in the blank).

Personally, my social skills have been shot since I was very young and developed only when I began working in retail at 16 y/o. I could “put on” a voice of Helper McHelperton and not be too concerned with “what should I say next” “did that sound weird” “was that offensive” type of thoughts. Harvesting dopamine at 26 made a big difference in my communication and at 27 I am still working on it. I connect better with older people than young people so I am not a good example. Although I think I was an extreme case of what is socially wrong with most young people now, it just hit me 10-15 years earlier than it did them. I really didn’t talk to anyone. Didn’t matter if they were my age or 99.

I recently saw a movie (yeah, I know but i had my Ras on) called “Us”. There was something like an upside-down where everyone living under the sun had a doppelgänger who lived in a bunker with artificial lighting. The doubles who lived underground couldn’t talk... they just grunted and paced around looking down. I wondered if the director was following Kruse, lol. The same reason why birds sing when the sun comes up is why all of those characters were mute.

I think most of the resistance of young people to connect with older people is due to low dopamine. When I peruse the web, trying on the eyes of a non-mito, I see a lot of subtle garbage aimed at dividing the generations that only adds to the physical effects of low dopamine.

Young people are obviously very concerned with protecting their “coolness” as well, which dictates who they will be seen associating with. Which is very uncool, and I think does not align with old folk’s experiences in coolness.

All I know is that shit is getting weird. You should have seen the face of this young lady driving her car as we passed her on the road yesterday. She was tweaked OUT. A beautiful, tall, Prince Charming type looking young dude came to check our oil tank and holy shit was he a debbie downer. All the older oil guys have been helpful and jolly. I do believe that older cats still carry the benefits of a less connected youth even if they are Facebook whores now. I find it so eerie that nearly an entire generation is so trashed in this regard. Im not sure if I mentioned in my journal that a 2 year old twin froze to death in my town this past winter because she was left outside overnight crying to come in. Neighbors heard the cries but couldn’t see anything outside from their window so did nothing. This was a stones throw from the fire station’s EMF soup. There is a lot that low dopamine people can’t see, including the wisdom they are missing out on by forgoing connections with older people.

Matt posed a question in the recent podcast with Jack, Luke Storey and that other guy that had my eyes bulging. He asked who he was expected to find as a suitable mate to bear children with, the little Mayan girls on the beach in PDC? Maybe, because the girl next door is probably cooking herself in WiFi soup.

### Penny — 2019-04-10T19:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-267786>

I was insane when I was young - not one iota of dopamine - I much prefer this me to my former self - I am retired and there is never enough time in the day to do all the things I want to do - at the age of 60 I am learning the guitar and playing in 2 ensembles - it ain't easy but I have found nothing worthwhile ever is -

### Christine_L — 2019-04-11T14:59:33+0300

I agree! Not to sound condescending but I mostly learn more from people who are older than me and give me that advice, experience and PERSPECTIVE I dont have. People have always told me I sound older than I am.

As for the younger people... sometimes their parents instill in them "oh you dont want to hang out with us old people" from an early age, so then they dont. If your family encourages it, youll most likely do it. After awhile maybe the young get scared of "old-ness" and dont want to come off as stale or old themselves. Its fear based, not love. Once you get to a different maturity its a million times easier.

You rock Caroline!

He asked who he was expected to find as a suitable mate to bear children with, the little Mayan girls on the beach in PDC? Maybe, because the girl next door is probably cooking herself in WiFi soup.

Ah, yes the age-old generational divide (pardon the pun). Young vs old. It's always been around, always will be. But it varies a lot with cultures.

In other times and places the aged were revered for their wisdom and experience, but not so much today. I was fortunate to have a beloved grandmother live in our house when we were growing up. She brought much joy to our childhood, and no-- she didn't try to act 'young' like some of today's seniors but instead acted her age with dignity.

Maybe I am just an old dinosaur now, but I am deeply disappointed with our latest generation incarnation-- Millennials. I don't disparage them individually (I have known quite a few in the workplace) but as a group they seem to a fitting cherry on a dysfunctional society.

**Millennial men** in particular are a puzzle (of course I am speaking in _generalities_ here-- so take this with a grain of salt).

Scientists have even coined a term "_The Hipster Effect_" to describe how, by rebelling against societal norms (in appearance). they have created their own conformity rules as evidenced by their regulation **soy boy uniform**: full beards, flannel shirts, skinny jeans, and so on. And also their preference for cool (everything they like is referred to as **_cool_**, which is really not cool) stuff like craft beer, designer coffee, latest iGadget, avocado toast, and so on. They and their peer women are into _cool_ digital social stuff, but they couldn't even fix something simple like a clogged toilet if their lives depended on it. Not cool.

Millennials are supposed to be a _Hero Generation_ (comparable to WWII generation) according to Strauss-Howe ' "4th Turning" generational theory but I call out BS on this.

Still, I know they are facing challenges we never faced, like world-wide proliferation of toxic RF/MW radiation, especially 5G. And, _gulp_, they are the future of humanity now.

So anyway, the generations in reflection are like passengers on a train. Some have been riding a while and are almost at their arrival destination (old people at the end of their lives), while others are just now boarding the train (young people). Let's hope the new and seasoned passengers benefit from each other's company while they are traveling together.

### Christine_L — 2019-04-15T17:53:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-time-a-beautiful-combination.23370/post-268104>

[@ElectricUniverse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21203/) if you think Millenials are bad, wait for Gen Z!!!

BTW I am a Millenial. I find that since we had half online culture and half real life culture pre-cell phones, its a mixed bag. Our parents (not mine) spoiled them up so much that they had everything given to them. Doesnt work, get mad, buy a new thing. Dont have to learn anything to "fix things" thats what you pay people to do. No need to buy a house, go travel everyday of your life and try not to work. There's some good things about being innovative and non-conventional and being flexible with living arrangements. Its easier to pack up and be nomadic in a 5G world then... if you know it exists! But know there are some good ones out there that are waking up to the paradigm! But theres a ton of soy boys LOL I will say that home ownership was not possible for a lot of us... getting married at a later age or not at all delays having children which really makes you wake up to reality!

Good comments.

I don't blame Millennials individually for their lot-- they just happened to be born into rapidly changing world (and much of it not for the better).

I have also heard that as a group Millennials are supposed to be looking more for 'experiences' and adventure than fitting into a standard cultural paradigm of working, saving, accumulating capital and acquiring the _American dream_ (a place you can call home sweet home). Thus they rent things when they need them, like cars (Lyft, Uber, and what have you). That may be great when you are young, footloose, and fancy free but as you get older some sense of permanence and financial security makes sense. They will find out the hard way ...

Sadly, many are also deep in debt right out of the gate of Life's race because they bought the "a college degree is the only way to get ahead" canard. So for many debt slavery starts very early.

# Thread: Key lessons for ladies to get down really quick on the journey to OPTIMAL
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/key-lessons-for-ladies-to-get-down-really-quick-on-the-journey-to-optimal.23371/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-23T15:24:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/key-lessons-for-ladies-to-get-down-really-quick-on-the-journey-to-optimal.23371/post-266269>

When you loved someone in your past and had to let them go, there will always be that small part of yourself that whispers, "**What was it that you wanted and why didn't you fight for it?
**
Make it a rule of life never to regret and never to look back. **Regret is an appalling waste of energy & TIME**; you can't build on it; it's only good for wallowing in.

Perspective: I’ve been let out of my cage, the only thing I’ve known in my adult life. It’s a binary choice.

Follow the good. Replicate the experiment in its entirety - people - sun - water.

_**Destroy the cage**_\- metaphorical, mentally, physically, socially, I don’t care. Just fucking do it RIGHT NOW.

Every woman that finally figured out her true worth in this life after dealing with life struggles, has picked up her suitcases of pride and boarded a flight to freedom, which always lands in the valley of change. This change in your mind must occur before your body and life change. Never forget this.

There comes a time in your life when you have to choose to turn the page, write another book or simply close it.

The wise among you will realize change is easy when you value your time well. This is why I highly recommend demolishing your "life bridges" you traveled in your past..........because then there is no choice but to move forward in a new way. Your past is solid, your future is liquid and it will take the form of the container you CHOOSE. Your reality can only be built by the NOW. Start using it wisely.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-26T12:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/key-lessons-for-ladies-to-get-down-really-quick-on-the-journey-to-optimal.23371/post-266468>

Ladies, please listen up.
The women that you try to talk to this about that hurt the most are your family and friends. Trust me on this because I am feeling that punch now. The formula is easy to implement get out in the AM sun. When the sun goes down do not turn on blue light devices without screen protection. Even with screen protection if the device is bright it still may destroy your melatonin. Get to bed as early as possible. Use blackout curtains in your rooms. Try and live in a rural area.

⬆️ Artificial light at night ups breast cancer risk by 73%.
Women have more melatonin sensitivity, so they're more impacted by light at night.
Just 0.2 lux (that's less light than 1 candle or the moon) can up tumor growth and bone damage.
[https://medicalxpress.com/news/2019-03-breast-cancer-likelier-bone-nighttime.html](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2019-03-breast-cancer-likelier-bone-nighttime.html)

# Thread: HOW DOES THE SUN CONTROL HEALING:  MITOCHONDRIAC LESSON
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-the-sun-control-healing-mitochondriac-lesson.23372/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-23T15:37:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-the-sun-control-healing-mitochondriac-lesson.23372/post-266271>

Sluggish autophagy is a sign of altered Calcium ion flows in your cell due to a lack of control of voltage gates by solar EMF. The most common cause of the lack of control is nnEMF which allows too much calcium efflux. This alters oxygen levels, the NAD+ couple, and free radical signaling in mitochondria to lead to higher heteroplasmy and disease tied to leptin resistance. Usually, melanopsin damage occurs as a TOPOLOGIC collateral effect of the light stress as the nnEMF.

HOW DOES THE PHYSICS LOOK FOR SLUGGISH AUTOPHAGY?

Ions in any static and uniform magnetic field will typically move in a circle with a cyclotron frequency determined by its charge q, its mass m, and the strength of the magnetic field called B. The operation of a cyclotron is based on the fact that the period of the motion of a charged particle in a uniform magnetic field is independent of the velocity of the particle, as can be seen in the following mathematical derivation:

F = ma

qvB = mv2/r

Solving for r:

r = mv/qB

Now find the period, T:

T = 1/f = 2π/ω = 2πr/v = 2πmv/qBv

Since the velocities' cancel:

T = 2πm/qB

The “Cyclotron Frequency” f (c) immediately follows and is:

F© = qB/2πm

These ion cyclotron resonance effects were extensively investigated in Abe Blackman’s lab but elsewhere in the 1980s up until today. All laboratories who have studied these effects found that calcium cyclotron frequency directly affects calmodulin regulation of the calcium ion concentration in solution and a host of biologic functions. These are all tied to marked alterations of voltage-dependent calcium channels when the field strength is above 30 Hz. To date, 23 studies have been done and repeated this finding since 1980. Anything that affects calmodulin by definition affects magnesium ion concentrations as well in a cell. This is why an altered electric and magnetic field by nnEMF's are primordial to calcium and magnesium regulation. Taking more calcium or magnesium do nothing for the intracellular voltage channels. Using calcium channel blockers, however, can help. What is the best calcium channel on Earth? **SUNLIGHT**

My engineer and neuroscience daughter and I have a fundamental difference on how we evaluate the information and that is the barrier that prohibits her from really even looking at any of this information. She believes that "consensus opinion of experts" has ultimate value versus "anecdotal evidence"...so she uses a logical fallacy of Appeal to Authority. I on the other hand consider myself to be the authority in Me, and attempt to use facts and logic and experimentation rather than opinion and consensus to inform my decision making. I was probably that way at one point in my life; I think that I am wiser now. As Dan said, have to be the example (and even that doesn't seem to be good enough ;-).

### drezy — 2019-07-18T17:20:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-the-sun-control-healing-mitochondriac-lesson.23372/post-274523>

There are portions of the book Antifragile by Nassim Taleb that lay this issue out really well and amazingly elegantly. There is a section in the book titled "First Principle of Iatrogenics (Empiricism)" in Chapter 21. It's well worth reading just that section in these situations in order to frame your own situation and get a well deserved sanity check.

In many respects the burden of proof for harm has been inverted over what would be logical. It is often assumed that anyone leery of new un-natural substances or technologies has the burden of proof squarely on them.

Personally I'm convinced that if the technology in some way enables more or better access t funny cat videos OR a substance has enough commercials with people in flowery fields then they will be totally accepted by most people instantly and the doubters viewed as backwards.

My wife and I are both engineers. There is hope.

**The world today is run by amoral people whom the gods have driven insane.**

It is sad to be here in this retarded clown show we call society, but there you have it.

It is all a cosmic drama, a game really, and we have to play it-- like it or not.

**I choose to filter everything I see and hear through a sanity test** that relies on common sense, experience, and intuition. It rarely fails me, even though there is little to do with your conclusions when you realize the reality of what is behind the curtain.

**Accept absolutely nothing at face value or at par for status quo**, not even your own beliefs and assumptions. To use a quote by former Intel CEO Andy Groves in a different context: "To survive you have to be paranoid".

For balance and to keep life worth living, focus your life actions and goals on truth, beauty, and goodness. They are all around you despite world's chimeric appearances.

Jack's message: Every morning you need the sun to charge separate water in your body to create the battery that creates your life. Melatonin teaches us a great many things about all the proteins in us that are biogenic amines that also work with sunlight in novel ways. The end result of this transaction for all four is to deliver energy to mitochondria in a novel way.

Almost 80% of T3 at the paraventricular nucleus originates from the peripheral conversion of T4 in cells outside the brain. Only 20% of hypothalamic T3 crosses the blood–brain barrier directly from the periphery. This makes T3 much more important in the human brain. Melanin and DOPA can be used to stimulate T3 production in the brain even when the thyroid is defective or missing.

The more one can tan the better this ability is. Most hypothyroid/diabetic patients are horrible at tanning because their skin is atrophic due to a lack of alpha, beta, and gamma MSH. Regular exposure to solar UV light via your eyes and skin stimulates your thyroid and brain to make T3, which balances your body's metabolism. Tanning increases your metabolism, which in turn helps you maintain a healthier weight. This is all done via POMC biophysics. Anything that blocks it fattens you and ruins your thyroid function.

T3 is the biophysical manna of longevity for mammals. T3 function is the best predictor of longevity for the human myocardium and CNS/PNS. These are the two tissues that kill humans the most. I built my house with protection from EMF and WIFI in 2011 after having been over exposed from past house with mold issues, my story is long, and my familly history is not very good in terms of disease so I've been doing everything I can to protect myself and my familly as my son was born in mold and EMF with old house as well, he's doing much better but I know he has serious issues with mitochondria, got it from me and all school environnement with cell tower right on school and more towers around it.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) 23 and me gives me MTHFR/C677T is +/+, MTHFR/A1298C is -/-, VDR/Taq1 is -/-, VDR/Fok1 is +/-, CBS/C699T is +/-, CBS/A360A is +/-, CBS/N212N is -/-, I live in Montreal Quebec Canada, well by your teachings I'm not at a good place, but can you tell me where would it be good for me?

Gina

March in the world of this Black Swan always brings the largest storms. I love the chaos of storms in my life. the march and April webinars have disrupted the comfort of many. GOOD. That was the point. I am a hurricane. Jeremy is a hurricane too. On that boat as the sunset, after 8 hours of talk about time with JT I decided to unleash hell yet again in March.

There is nothing else than now. There is neither yesterday, certainly, nor is there any tomorrow. How old must you be before you know that? Well, I think the wise can learn it at any age.

In my own life, some people are hurricanes to me and they blow big storms toward me every so often to test me. Instead of fighting them, I decided 15 March’s ago, I might as well enjoy 'their' discomfort. It might ruin everything I’ve built up til now, but so be it —— I still think I should enjoy it as she resculpts my landscape. Things have a way of changing in my life just about every March and I am beginning to get used to it. My caterpillar needs to get its butterfly to gain its wings to fly to new adventures in my life. I've decided not to waste any more of my time. My wishes are no longer just for me anymore, my wishes now are for 'us' and the future destruction I need to bring to the world. I like how destruction leads to new empires and ideas.

So when I awoke today Alex left me this on the table at KLC..........

for those of you out there who have no idea what is brewing.......here is a clue. Remain quiet and watch the actions.........and ignore the words you might hear.

Uncle Jack is brewing some new magic.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-31T18:19:24+0300

My quote on my Kruse Longevity Center page contains the 'Tainted' word because of Alex and Dustin. I am now entangled with them both for eternity. Sometimes we have to take a few steps back in life to make the quantum leap forward in our lives to gain the progress we seek in the journey to optimal. I love the relentless chaos of this leap in my life. I intend to infect my flock of Black Swans with this wisdom I have collected the past 15 years. Never settle for less than you deserve and always jump before you have your wings. When you do, you'll see how Nature always provides your answer on the way to your new life. Never settle because this creates regrets and one should never have any before your own death.

Challenge yourself to be who you know you are capable of being. Challenge yourself to live what you preach, to walk your talk and reveal in your version of crazy. They will be drawn to you like bugs to light.

A fall from great innocence hurts the most because you know that you are now tainted. Many things open the door to heaven and hell. Tainted love appears to be a hell that may have ascended or heaven may have descended only to prove what we carry in our mind is an exaggerated memory of an imagined beautiful love.

Never cry for me........Uncle Jack knows exactly what he is doing. He is living fully. I know where the pieces fit. You do not.

What are photobiomodulation and photosensitizer therapy for mitochondrial colony optimization? Sounds quite unusual to the non-Black Swan, doesn't it? I use these potions with light frequencies to create Black Swans. Alex and Dustin just left me at the Center this AM as they drive to the beach to set sail for the new life together.......“All that is gold does not glitter, Not all those who wander are lost.” So what was in the rivers of IV's we used and the light frequencies we danced around those mitochondria? The WISDOM of nature. No person ever steps in the same river twice, for it's not the same river and they are not the same person. Here are Alex and Dustin this AM below at the beach..........as they head to the sun of the Emerald Coast in the panhandle looking to change their fortunes.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-31T18:31:18+0300

So the idea of mitigating artificial light, nnEMF, food and heating in the winter isn't going to go down well with the masses, but eating these foreign foods, foreign heating and foreign light is just taking away the protection, the body armour, the resilience, the safety belt, the natural ability we've developed to fight off all that we're now trying to cure with pharmaceuticals ... it's a dead end game from what I understand

It all comes back to light: every time.......

My tribe are Black Swans. They never stop trying to understand the struggle to get Optimal.

Now I want to respond to my tribe here since the latest two members left the nest this AM. This Center is MY DREAM. Sometimes dreams need to be paused. Sometimes one must take two steps backward to make a Quantum leap forward. NEVER BE AFRAID OF THIS. Many of you do not seem to understand or appreciate this. My two new Black Swans do implicitly. It is about time some of you begin to embrace this.

It is exactly what you need.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-03-31T18:40:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-entanglement-continues-with-our-latest-members-at-the-farm-dustin-and-alex.23425/post-266942>

I won't dumb nature down, ever for you or anyone else. It won’t happen on my watch because I want to educate and elevate people and not keep them dumbed down. I abhor the low dopamine mindset of modern man. For those of you who wanted it dumbed down” go to another universe where the rules are more to your liking. I am not sharing my wisdom here or anywhere else to become pleasing to the masses. I am doing it to uncover where Optimal health really lies for me and for you. you can take those morsels you collect here and do with them what you like.

My Farm members get much larger pieces of the pie of understanding than you will collect here. When they leave they really understand what this means.

They begin to understand my passion around valuing time.......theirs and mine.

I will unleash hell on myself, my misfits, and my tribe until they VALUE time properly.

Most people really don’t want the truth, they just want constant reassurance that what they believe is the truth. My mouth is not a bakery. I don’t sugarcoat anything. If you ask me my opinion I’m going to tell you the truth, no matter what you want to hear. There is a general difference between the physical and the social sciences that people ought to remember. In the social sciences, if you don't like “it”, often you have at least some space to change “it”. Some wiggle room. eg. if you don't like the language, you can change the language. If you don't like the political system, you change the political system. There is nothing to measure it against how effective these things are, because the fact that people want them is itself the measure of their effectiveness. Nature gives a rats ass about your wants needs and desires and that is how I teach her brand of science. So it is important to remember that in the physical sciences, you DO have something to measure things against. I believe this is the sentiment a teacher ought to express to his students when teaching them about the physics of organisms in nature. People sometimes seem to think that they can negotiate how they want the physical world to be like, or the rules we are going to use to measure it. The fact is, we can't use what we 'want' there anymore. Whether it fits the world, if it's accurate or not accurate, becomes the only matter of importance. It is time you wake up to this reality and elevate your game to Nature’s rules.

**Leadership is the activity of influencing people to cooperate toward some goal which they come to find desirable after they have been re-educated by nature.

I SPECIALIZE IN CREATING THOSE EMOTIONAL IMPRINTS IN THE HEARTS OF MY SWANS.

**

I don't think 99.99999% of people you reach really grasp how **_not_** controversial your framework is -- if this was any other area besides diet-land, there wouldn't even be any room for push-back against you because there's absolutely nothing "crazy" at all about anything that you're talking about.

The major way it is done wirelessly with terrestrial sunlight is this: When a cathode ray hits an anode free electrons are liberated from the anode's surface. This is why humans ARE THE ONLY primate that has sweat glands on their hands and feet. We are not tree dwellers who are always grounded to Earth, therefore, nature gave us an added advantage to offset our life on Earth since we are bipedal. The human breast is also a modified human sweat gland for electron transfer between mother and infants mitochondria for the same brain reasons. Those free electrons are liberated according to the sunlight barcode Fraunhofer lines which vary as the sun delivers its wireless information to Earth and life on this planet. This is how light is used photoelectrically and photoacoustically to power and INFORM life give it information to organize cells and to drive body plan to build out via cymatics by changing the light to sound wave which can be controlled magnetically by the Earth magnetic field. You must hire experts who know the basics of how life organizes to maintain health. This is critical in the organization of the infant and child brain. **Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 3

### Jack Kruse — 2019-04-03T22:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/round-table-bio-hacking-session-from-los-angeles-california-12-14-18.23449/post-267240>

Overheard on the net, "I laughed during at the latest podcast, Kruse, Luke y’all my god the BEST podcast of all time!
I love Jack's perspective. He's so raw & real. To hear him "low voltage" was fun too.
“You worries about your bones?”
“I’m worried about your boner!”
What do y’all think of this episode?
Should I do more of these?

### OliverGruener2 — 2019-04-05T14:41:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/round-table-bio-hacking-session-from-los-angeles-california-12-14-18.23449/post-267386>

Yes! Do more like these

I really enjoyed it.

### Katie Durham — 2019-04-09T15:07:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/round-table-bio-hacking-session-from-los-angeles-california-12-14-18.23449/post-267674>

Jack, your comments in the podcast about how oxygen therapy (eg, hyperbaric oxygen) isn't always a good idea was a head slap to me. 8 years ago my husband had bypass surgery at a top Boston hospital following a heart attack. A few nights later a battleaxe nurse put him on oxygen all night. He felt himself going downhill and begged to be taken off of it, but no go until the change of nurses in the morning. Hubby decided the problem was he was allergic to it, and was sure because he had read something like that once. Because he was on Adderall for his ADHD, a house psychiatrist had to meet with him. The young psych jerk comes sailing into his room a couple days later when I was there, and as he enters condescendingly says "so, talk to me about oxygen allergies...". I wanted to throw him out the window for making my husband feel so small and scared and cornered, at a time he still felt like complete shit from the operation.

So you make your comment and I realize it was what I had just been reading about recently, reperfusion injury. Googling I find that it is a major cause of fatality following bypass surgery and that it was well known long before his surgery. Easy for me to understand now, how if there is any backup in the ECT it can lead to severe inflammation. Hubby had been making good progress, but then after the night of O2 he went downhill and developed congestive heart failure. Maybe it would have developed anyway but this may have made it more pronounced and hastened his demise a few years later. For all he was already dealing with, his redox must have been rock bottom. Knowing what I know now, it wouldn't have made a difference because he wouldn't have listened to me, but so many of his doctors just made it worse.

Thank you so much for all you have shared.

Ideology much?

# Thread: Redox is a function of TIME and time is built by the light we allow.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-is-a-function-of-time-and-time-is-built-by-the-light-we-allow.23477/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2019-04-09T14:29:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-is-a-function-of-time-and-time-is-built-by-the-light-we-allow.23477/post-267670>

Most of you know Jeremy and I are best friends. He runs mohawksteel and he took a snapshot of me on the boat on March 24, 2019, of that infamous March Q & A. When we first met he told me about he revered Thomas Paine's manifesto and I realized I have found someone who valued time as much as I do.

Immediately we became kin.

He posted this on IG recently to time mark our epic day together and I have to post it here.

Redox is a function of time.

My timeless friend [@drjackkruse](https://www.instagram.com/drjackkruse/) has shared soooo much with me, I could never be the same man after knowing him. I stole his face for this post because it is he and PeterD who have contributed most to my Philosophy of Time. [#knowYourHeroes](https://www.instagram.com/explore/tags/knowyourheroes/)[#entangled](https://www.instagram.com/explore/tags/entangled/) [#friends](https://www.instagram.com/explore/tags/friends/)[#spendYourDimeOnTime](https://www.instagram.com/explore/tags/spendyourdimeontime/)

For those of you who do not know Peter D was the best salvage diver on Earth. He was JT and my friend. He had stage 4 lung CA diagnosed years ago. We met at my house years ago and came to an agreement that we would write a blog called three men dying. We did it because we wanted to show our friends and families how we valued time and why time was really relative. Peter was dying faster than me or JT. JT was dying next quickest because of his own diagnosis. I was the one who was dying at the slowest pace because I had nothing wrong with me except my blue light toxic and nnEMF life in neurosurgery. The blog is very private and has amazing insights from all of us. Peter D died and the blog kinda lost steam because of how his death affected me and JT.

Recently that blog has picked up steam. It is now about two men dying at their relative paces. Recently, I realized that I was dying at a faster rate than I anticipated because of drama going on in my own family. So I took JT on a boat and we talked about Peter and life on the boat........a boat that, that if the sea sunk Peter could have salvaged like no one's business.

This 10 hour day was one of the best days of my life because JT explained to me I could slow my death down rapidly if I just lived authentically once again and stopped letting people around me drain me.

He told me he needed me optimal to help him save his own time. And he reminded me that all three of us promised one another that we would protect each others time when we agreed to pen our thoughts on time for the blog. It refocused me and my life immediately. In a few weeks since this time I have become razor sharp and I have completely blown up my life to align with my philosophy and my values. It also has ignited my passion and purpose for my mission as I live out my days on this EARTH.

Here is the URL of the post:
[https://www.instagram.com/p/BwAFxvpHJvA/](https://www.instagram.com/p/BwAFxvpHJvA/)

JT of mohawksteel said this on IG:
**[mohawksteel](https://www.instagram.com/mohawksteel/)**
The most powerful spiritual and philosophical moment in my life was when I read the first page of Thomas Paine’s “Common Sense”. The feeling grew until I thought myself on fire and nearly collapsed as I read this last sentence. I was 19, it has shaped my life every day since.
....“Time makes more converts than reason” -Thomas Paine
In other words: Time is more convincing than reason…
If reason and time were two different people…
**Reason would fear Time** …
Time would not fear Reason
We seek to quantify and collect time as if every second held equal value.
The pennies that makeup dollars are this way, accumulation is wealth.
Time has its own standard of wealth, seconds could be worth fortunes.
fortunes could be lost in seconds, we cling to health as the key to wealth.
But it is not… neither is the collection time… Instead:
**Spend your dime on time worth collecting.**
Seek the things that give time more value.
Love of all flavors, Adventure, Friendship, Gratitude
Never plateau… never settle
Never believe the answer is worth more than the experience.
Loosen your grip on the clock of logic.
You may shape the day with the chisel and hammer…
But, our boldest strokes come when we are thinking about time.
Reason waits to be proven wrong...
Time decides everything.

JT is an amazing human being and he is my favorite misfit I have collected in this life to date.

You all need to find JT's in your life.............

It is the most critical key in an Optimal journey to health, wellness, and life.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-04-09T14:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redox-is-a-function-of-time-and-time-is-built-by-the-light-we-allow.23477/post-267671>

Time decides who you meet in your life, your heart decides who you want in your life, and your behavior decides who stays in your life. [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...nd-time-is-built-by-the-light-we-allow.23477/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/redox-is-a-function-of-time-and-time-is-built-by-the-light-we-allow.23477/)

# Thread: What does a hug mean to you.......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-a-hug-mean-to-you.23483/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2019-04-10T04:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-a-hug-mean-to-you.23483/post-267754>

I suppose I didn't always appreciate hugs ....fully anyway.

My partner, unquestionably, gives the best hugs I have ever had. When Tony wraps me in a big bear hug I feel I am protected and loved and cared for. This hug doesn't ask for or demand anything...it does tell me I am so loved and cared for and safe - no matter what.

I have talked to a number of women about hugs from their significant other and it seems sometimes a hug from their guy has an ulterior motive. It is only about sex. no Bueno btw.

I love a hug with no borders, no restrictions ......The only intention is to show ultimate love and or concern and joy.

When you get a wonderful hug .....this person, the huger, really "sees" you. They see your beauty, your pain, your sorrow, your joy, your wishes and desires.

A hug is a very beautiful thing ......

Are you able to hug? or is it something you have had to learn.....

### recoen — 2019-04-10T05:30:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-a-hug-mean-to-you.23483/post-267756>

I don’t like the “fake half-hug” many acquaintances (even those meeting for the first time) engage in. I only hug those who I have a deep connection with. The true masculine embracing the true feminine, as you allude to above, are hugs in their best form .

### drezy — 2019-04-10T16:07:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-a-hug-mean-to-you.23483/post-267768>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=267754)
>
> Are you able to hug? or is it something you have had to learn.....

I learned from my maternal grandma. She was an awesome grandma and taught me how to put exactly the amount of smoosh-factor into a hug to be just this side of distressing. I remember thinking "OK! OK! I got it! You love me!" but I (and perhaps the shape my ribcage) will never ever forget.

I've set a policy to have 3 hug settings:
full hug
pressurized full hug (scientifically calibrated)
pressurized full hug with lift
Note -goosing is optional and highly context dependent

My wife's family was not physically affectionate. I had to teach her and still retrain her every morning. It's built into her personality that she'll start everyday reviewing a checklist of things we need todo first thing. I remind her almost every morning "Nope, aint going to happen with a hug!" or if I'm feeling particularly ripped off by the lack of hug I'll look attentively into her eyes (very sensitive male style) and listen to the entire list and follow up with "You know what? Nobody gave me a hug yet! It all went in one ear and right out the other!" I won't settle until I get a pressurized hug that meets specification.

### Nadya — 2019-04-10T16:17:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-a-hug-mean-to-you.23483/post-267769>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=267768)
>
> I learned from my maternal grandma. She was an awesome grandma and taught me how to put exactly the amount of smoosh-factor into a hug to be just this side of distressing. I remember thinking "OK! OK! I got it! You love me!" but I (and perhaps the shape my ribcage) will never ever forget.
>
> I've set a policy to have 3 hug settings:
> full hug
> pressurized full hug (scientifically calibrated)
> pressurized full hug with lift
> Note -goosing is optional and highly context dependent
>
> My wife's family was not physically affectionate. I had to teach her and still retrain her every morning. It's built into her personality that she'll start everyday reviewing a checklist of things we need todo first thing. I remind her almost every morning "Nope, aint going to happen with a hug!" or if I'm feeling particularly ripped off by the lack of hug I'll look attentively into her eyes (very sensitive male style) and listen to the entire list and follow up with "You know what? Nobody gave me a hug yet! It all went in one ear and right out the other!" I won't settle until I get a pressurized hug that meets specification.

Love it!

### drezy — 2019-04-11T01:46:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-a-hug-mean-to-you.23483/post-267816>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=267774)
>
> Love it !

In retrospect so much became clearer. Never underestimate the underdog or someone who is behind the score.

My grandma's mother died giving birth to her brother before my grandma was 5. The chance that her Swiss immigrant working class father could have ever matched the hug count of a mother is near nil. She caught up in count and pressure with her grandkids though.

The oddest thing was that grandpa (her husband) had a messed up lonely childhood away from his parents. He never had toys as a kid, but as a 60 yo he made sure to keep stocked up on the best building blocks and other building toys.

So when my mom would ask "Would you like to go to grandma's?" my response was always "(F\*ck) Yeah!" -- OK I didn't quite have the full vocabulary at that point. One almost painful hug, an infinite supply of fresh baked cinnamon buns, and a grandpa ready to jump down on the floor and build crazy stuff with me with me. No brainer for a kid. I had the equivalent of rockstar grandparents.

One big takeaway, don't half-ass your hugs.

### caroline — 2019-04-11T04:01:59+0300

### Christine_L — 2019-04-12T00:23:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-a-hug-mean-to-you.23483/post-267881>

I am a believer of big, real hugs. I try my best to envelope my friends! Thankfully my BF is a huge hugger. He's very tall and has huge wingspan. I love it!

### caroline — 2019-04-12T08:38:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-a-hug-mean-to-you.23483/post-267894>

I had almost forgotten .....the very best hugs I have ever gotten BT \[before Tony\] were from a bell hop in a hotel in Florida. I had left Stella and Jack and was getting ready to catch a plane back to Oz.

This young man was the most remarkable hugger and I just had to ask him about it. He said that hugging was hugely important to him - but they had to be real and last a long time!.

I asked him if he had a girlfriend and if she was a hugger......He told me that he could never be with anyone who didn't love to hug ...and, yes, she is a great hugger.

So I had to pass this along to my step son when he started seeing a lovely young woman \[who is now his wife\]

I told him to never be with anyone who doesn't love hugging as much as you do ....and definitely never be with anyone who doesn't love sex as much as you do! He said ...."check and check" I got it covered!

And yes .....I do have several photos of this lovely young bell hop! He was totally gorgeous.

This young man had the most caring and lovely and loving nature ....I will never forget him. he took care of me like I was gold. He then handed me over to an equally lovely cab driver who took me to an airport hotel. He didn't charge me - he wouldn't take any money and he took me into the hotel and made sure my reservation was okay and then gave me a hug goodbye.

and then .....the hotel maid took care of me. We wound up crying in each other's arms.

It was all very surreal .........what the heck was I projecting?

On the other side of the coin ....I got talking to a young man in the swimming pool and he needed sex advice ....go figure!

He adored his wife and loved making love to her ......but she held him at arms length way too often! I had seen Jack's sex webinar so I was able to give him lots of great advise! YAY me!!!

### Jack Kruse — 2019-04-12T14:35:38+0300

### caroline — 2019-04-13T06:23:29+0300

But ...... are you the first to break a hug????

### caroline — 2019-04-13T06:28:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-does-a-hug-mean-to-you.23483/post-267978>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=267904)
>
> Hugs have become a new mantra for me. I gave out a lot of them for the last 6 weeks.
>
> Does your circle of six have respect and trust? If not change it today.
